On Wed, Oct 28, 2026 at 08:14 AM Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week — the Dedicated cluster looked promising...



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Async runs - request for SLO tolerance and idempotency confirmation

Hi all,

I dug into the workload_summary.csv. Quick read: 72% small (<30s), 22% medium (30s–6m), 6% large (>6m). Peak concurrent tasks observed in your sample: ~9k. With Dedicated capacity we can hit these throughput numbers but need to be explicit about failure modes and dedupe. Proposal below:

Operational SLO proposal (temporary carveout):
- Target: 99% of jobs complete within 12 hours, 99.95% within 24 hours, 100% within 48 hours (credit-triggered if 24hr 99.95% missed).
- Rationale: gives buffer for bursty nights and hardware maintenance windows; 48hr final bound avoids unbounded tail.

Retries & idempotency requirements:
- Require an Idempotency-Token header (Idempotency-Token: <uuid-v4>) on job submission; tokens are scoped per-job-id.
- Server dedupe window: 72 hours for token-based dedupe (tokens expire after 72h). After expiry a replay is treated as a new job.
- Delivery semantics: at-least-once for the async queue; with the token dedupe you get effective once semantics for processing. Clients MUST be prepared for duplicates within the dedupe window for external side effects.
- Recommended retry/backoff: exponential backoff starting at 30s, max backoff 20m, with jitter; controller should stop retries after 6 attempts and escalate with a failure webhook.

Monitoring and credits:
- We'll expose per-job lifecycle events in the Console (submission, started, completed, failed). We can create a route-level SLO monitor and an alert that computes 24h completion percent.
- For the temporary carveout we propose a credit schedule: if 24h 99.95% misses by >0.5%, 5% service credit for the affected billing month; >2% miss -> 15% credit and a mandatory root cause review.

Operational caveats:
- The credit policy assumes Dedicated reserved capacity with the configured autoscaling policy (we'll attach recommended pool sizing). If you're on shared Hosted capacity the tail can be longer; we recommend Dedicated for this workload.

I'll attach a draft operational_runbook.md and pool-sizing notes. If this looks reasonable Ben, I'll turn it into a short doc for legal.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

Attachments: operational_runbook.md (md)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, contracts@acme.ai
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2026 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Async runs - request for SLO tolerance and idempotency confirmation

Maya — following up with a consolidated response and next steps. We've reviewed Marcus' proposal and are prepared to approve a timeboxed SLO tolerance for Nightly Enrich V2. Summary below: 

Approved temporary SLO carveout (effective immediately through 2027-01-31):
- 99% complete within 12 hours; 99.95% within 24 hours; final completion guarantee within 48 hours.
- Dedupe window: Idempotency-Token header required; dedupe window 72 hours.
- Retry expectations: at-least-once delivery; clients must supply Idempotency-Token; recommended controller retry policy as Marcus suggested.
- Credits: the credit schedule Marcus proposed will be included in the carveout addendum.

Next steps: 
1) Marcus will deliver the operational_runbook.md and pool-sizing estimate by Wed 11/3.
2) Laura will draft the carveout addendum (proposed_slo_carveout.docx) and route it to ACME's contracts team for signature.
3) Ben/ACME will configure the first-week trace-enabled run so we can validate metrics and tune autoscaling.

If ACME is comfortable with the terms we can map this to an amendment to the Dedicated contract. Contracts@acme.ai — please confirm who will sign on your side; I'll have Laura share a DocuSign link once the doc is ready.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: Phase-gated prepay incentive request — Catapult Health
preview:
From: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Hi Kimberly + Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the throughput and latency numbers look promising for our remote clinical summarization workload. Leadership asked me to explore a 2–3 year committed plan, but they want a structure that:

- Keeps year 1 cash outlay lower via phase-gated prepay (so we can prove production metrics)
- Gives escalating discounts if we hit certain throughput thresholds and maintain 99.7% SLA
- Allows a mid-term reallocation if utilisation skews heavily toward year 2

I've attached a simple spreadsheet with a proposed staging (Year1 40%, Year2 35%, Year3 25%) and suggested breakpoints. Can Redwood provide a formal proposal that includes: a) the discount tiers, b) true-up mechanics, and c) a short clawback for material underrun?

We'd like to present something to our CFO by Oct 20.

Best,
Sara
Sara Patel
Dir, Platform Ops — Catapult Health
attachments: Catapult-Phase-Gated-Spreadsheet.xlsx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Sara — thanks for the clear ask and the spreadsheet. Ben and I reviewed; this is the right kind of flexibility for health-tech buyers. A few quick clarifying Qs so we can scope the proposal:

1) When you say "phase-gated" — do you want firm cash prepay at the start of each year (but with defined release thresholds), or a single upfront prepay split by internal billing schedule?
2) Is Catapult open to a small uplift on unit price in exchange for a guaranteed annual renewal floor?
3) Which patent/data retention requirements should we flag for the Private deployment option (might affect discounts)?

We'll draft a two-option proposal (2-year accelerated and 3-year laddered) with explicit true-up windows and a 60–90 day clawback for underrun. Marcus, could you run a quick ROI sketch for both options?

Thanks — Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference — redwood.com

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05T14:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Attaching a short ROI note and two pricing vectors:

- 2-year Accelerated (A): Year1 45% prepay, Year2 55% prepay settlement; ~20% blended discount if Year1 hits 50% of committed tokens.
- 3-year Laddered (B): Year1 35% / Year2 35% / Year3 30%; step discounts at 60/85/110% of baseline usage (5/8/12% respectively).

I flagged a potential margin hit under A if year 2 doesn't materialize; recommend a 90-day true-up window and a clawback capped at 15% of credited discount. Laura — legal comments below on simple contract language.

Attachments: Draft-AML-Terms-v3.pdf
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-10-07T09:06:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Quick legal:
- Keep clawback language limited to material underrun defined as cumulative usage < 70% of committed over 12 months.
- Add a one-time mid-term reallocation clause: customers can reassign up to 20% of remaining year commitment across years without penalty (requires 45-day notice).
- For Public/Private deployment mix, require a security appendix to qualify for the top-tier discounts.

I'll draft standard clauses and mark optional toggles. Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Contracts & Privacy | Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-10-12T08:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Kimberly — thank you. High level from finance: Catapult prefers Year1 cash exposure <= 50% of the total committed consideration for FY1. They like the 3-year laddered because it reduces near-term cash. A few asks:

- Can Redwood provide a sliding discount that also accounts for peak-day throughput (we have bursty months)?
- Any flexibility on the cap for reallocation? Would 30% be reasonable?
- Can SLA-based credits be pre-funded as a small reserve?

We'd appreciate a clean redline we can share with our GC.

-Rajiv


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Rajiv / Sara — based on your feedback we've prepared an initial, conservative commercial offer:

Option B (recommended, 3-year laddered):
- Commitment: 3-year term, baseline annual quantity = 1.0x
- Billing: Year1 prepay 40%, Year2 prepay 35% invoiced at start of year, Year3 prepay 25%
- Discount schedule (applies to consumed tokens vs baseline): 0–60%: 0% discount; 60–85%: 6%; 85–110%: 10%; >110%: 14%
- Peak-day rider: measured peak-day throughput above baseline contributes to a separate burst pool with a 5% reduced unit uplift when aggregated monthly (helps burst months)
- Reallocation: up to 25% reallocation allowed (45-day notice) — no penalty for first reallocation; subsequent reallocations limited and subject to small admin fee
- Clawback/true-up: 90-day annual true-up; clawback capped at 12% of credits issued in previous year; material underrun defined as cumulative consumption < 68%
- Security appendix required for Private deployment discounts (signed addendum)

We can tighten the reallocation cap to 30% if Catapult moves to a 45% Year1 prepay instead of 40%. I'll attach a marked commercial term sheet and a draft redline. Can you confirm if your GC prefers DocuSign or a downloadable redline?

Next steps:
1) Catapult to confirm preferred Option (B vs A) and Year1 cash tolerance by Oct 26.
2) Redwood to send formal redline/DocuSign on Oct 27.

Thanks — Kimberly

Date: 2026-10-30T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Kimberly — appreciate the clear proposal. Catapult's CFO reviewed and we're broadly aligned with Option B. Two final asks before we proceed to GC:

1) Increase the >110% discount tier to 16% (we expect to grow quickly if early trials succeed).
2) Accept a 30% reallocation cap while keeping Year1 at 40% prepay (our treasury team prefers the lower upfront).

If you can accept both, we'll instruct our GC to prioritize review and request a DocuSign. Otherwise we may propose 45% Year1 to get 30% reallocation.

We'll wait for your confirmation so we can initiate DocuSign next week.

Regards,
Rajiv

Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Attaching a short ROI note and two pricing vectors:

- 2-year Accelerated (A): Year1 45% prepay, Year2 55% prepay settlement; ~20% blended discount if Year1 hits 50% of committed tokens.
- 3-year Laddered (B): Year1 35% / Year2 35% / Year3 30%; step discounts at 60/85/110% of baseline usage (5/8/12% respectively).

I flagged a potential margin hit under A if year 2 doesn't materialize; recommend a 90-day true-up window and a clawback capped at 15% of credited discount. Laura — legal comments below on simple contract language.

Attachments: Draft-AML-Terms-v3.pdf
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

- Add a one-time mid-term reallocation clause: customers can reassign up to 20% of remaining year commitment across years without penalty (requires 45-day notice).
- For Public/Private deployment mix, require a security appendix to qualify for the top-tier discounts.

Head of Contracts & Privacy | Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Kimberly — thank you. High level from finance: Catapult prefers Year1 cash exposure <= 50% of the total committed consideration for FY1. They like the 3-year laddered because it reduces near-term cash. A few asks:

- Can Redwood provide a sliding discount that also accounts for peak-day throughput (we have bursty months)?
- Any flexibility on the cap for reallocation? Would 30% be reasonable?
- Can SLA-based credits be pre-funded as a small reserve?

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>

Subject: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Rajiv / Sara — based on your feedback we've prepared an initial, conservative commercial offer:

Option B (recommended, 3-year laddered):
- Commitment: 3-year term, baseline annual quantity = 1.0x
- Billing: Year1 prepay 40%, Year2 prepay 35% invoiced at start of year, Year3 prepay 25%
- Discount schedule (applies to consumed tokens vs baseline): 0–60%: 0% discount; 60–85%: 6%; 85–110%: 10%; >110%: 14%
- Peak-day rider: measured peak-day throughput above baseline contributes to a separate burst pool with a 5% reduced unit uplift when aggregated monthly (helps burst months)
- Reallocation: up to 25% reallocation allowed (45-day notice) — no penalty for first reallocation; subsequent reallocations limited and subject to small admin fee
- Clawback/true-up: 90-day annual true-up; clawback capped at 12% of credits issued in previous year; material underrun defined as cumulative consumption < 68%
- Security appendix required for Private deployment discounts (signed addendum)

We can tighten the reallocation cap to 30% if Catapult moves to a 45% Year1 prepay instead of 40%. I'll attach a marked commercial term sheet and a draft redline. Can you confirm if your GC prefers DocuSign or a downloadable redline?

Next steps:
1) Catapult to confirm preferred Option (B vs A) and Year1 cash tolerance by Oct 26.
2) Redwood to send formal redline/DocuSign on Oct 27.

Thanks — Kimberly

Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Matt O'Neal <matt.oneal@clarionbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-29T08:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: pen-test chronology, SBOM export and remediation SLAs

Matt / Chloe — adding the technical specifics below and attaching the artifacts to the shared drive link. Quick summary first, then the SLA matrix.

Summary of pen test (internal/external engagement):
- Engagement window: 2026-09-20 to 2026-10-02
- Scope: hosted API ingress, dedicated inference control plane, model-serving runtime (no access to private customer data sets).
- Test type: external blackbox + authenticated internal scan; no social engineering activities were performed.
- Major findings: 2 critical (both memory isolation race conditions in a custom worker library — fixed), 5 high (authentication token expiry misconfigurations, minor SSRF vector) — all findings have remediation PRs merged or mitigations in place.
- Current status: Critical issues patched and verified (2026-10-12); high issues either mitigated or scheduled for patch in the next maintenance window (tracked in Jira SEC-7241).

Attachments uploaded to Drive (links included in package):
- clarionbank_pen_test_summary_20261015.pdf (executive + technical appendix)
- sast_dast_excerpts_oct2026.zip (redacted stack traces, repro steps for high/critical)
- redwood_sbom_manifest_20261020.csv (component, version, license, source)
- vm_remediation_sla_matrix.csv (breakdown below)

Patch SLA matrix (target windows from discovery/triage to prod fix and verification):
- Critical (remote RCE, memory corruption, token leakage): initial acknowledgement within 4 hours, mitigation or customer-facing workaround within 24 hours, production patch or hotfix deployed within 72 hours, verification & re-test within 7 days.
- High (privilege escalation, SSRF, auth issues): initial acknowledgement within 24 hours, remediation scheduled within 30 days, security verification within 14 days of fix deployment.
- Medium: acknowledged within 72 hours, remediation on regular monthly patch cadence (usually 60–90 days).
- Low: tracked for upcoming releases and for observability improvements.

Emergency hotfix workflow: 24/7 on-call security + infra runbook. For critical customer-impacting issues we open a P1 with immediate cross-functional response and a single POC (we'll include the POC contact in the attestation).

Compliance notes:
- SBOM generation: our CI pipeline emits SBOMs at build time with provenance tags and we retain SBOM snapshots per release for 12 months by default (longer on request for dedicated/private deployments).
- Pen test attestation: we provide an executive attestation signed by our Head of Security once ClarionBank confirms the package is sufficient for procurement.

Marissa — storage & delivery: please confirm the Drive path and that the attachments are accessible to matt.oneal@clarionbank.com.

Ben Carter
Principal Security Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments in message: clarionbank_pen_test_summary_20261015.pdf, redwood_sbom_manifest_20261020.csv, sast_dast_excerpts_oct2026.zip, vm_remediation_sla_matrix.csv

Subject: Re: Request: pen-test chronology, SBOM export and remediation SLAs

Chloe / Ben — received, thank you. We were able to pull the files from the Drive link and they look good at a first pass. A few quick follow-ups: 

1) Can you add the exact SHA256 hashes as an extra column in the SBOM CSV for binary provenance? Helps our SBOM tooling.
2) For the critical memory fixes — can we get the specific commit IDs and the short timeline (detected -> patch deployed) in the executive summary appendix?
3) We'll need the signed attestation for procurement. Please route via DocuSign to security@clarionbank.com and legal@clarionbank.com.

If you can push the SBOM CSV with hashes and the attestation today, we can close our evidence request and move to contract redline review.

Thanks — Matt

ClarionBank

title: Post-demo: cost & benchmarking follow-up (token volumes + $/1M targets)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emily Cho <emily@avahealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: cost & benchmarking follow-up (token volumes + $/1M targets)

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for taking the demo earlier today. Great discussion and really appreciated the specifics on how Ava ingests clinical notes and surfaces summaries for care teams. As promised, quick recap + a couple of clarifying asks so we can build a tailored pricing proposal.

Recap (2 lines):
- Primary use: clinical summarization + triage prompts for care ops (short prompts + 800–1,200 token responses typical).
- Expected cadence: pilot with ~150k WAUs, peaking into heavy inference windows at shift changes.

Asks to finalize pricing options:
1) Expected monthly token volume range for pilot and 6-month ramp (rough ranges are fine): pilot month 1, pilot month 3, steady-state month 6.
2) Target monthly budget range (ballpark) for the pilot and post-pilot (you mentioned procurement will set a monthly ceiling).
3) Any must-have procurement constraints (PO lead time, invoicing entity, SOC2/audit paperwork timing).

To help with benchmarking, we pulled quick unit comps vs common hosts (rough scale only): OpenAI/Anthropic tend to sit higher on hosted managed endpoints for high-quality chat/long responses; self-hosted/open-model hosts can be materially lower per-1M token but add infra/ops overhead. I’ll share two price tier options once you confirm volumes and budget.

Attached: demo slides and a simple pricing workbook with tiered $/1M scenarios.

Proposed immediate next step: if you can send the month-by-month token estimates and procurement timeline by end of day Friday, we’ll deliver a formal pilot proposal and a target $/1M benchmark for Redwood vs the hosts we referenced.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0123

Attachments: Ava_Demo_Slides.pdf (pdf), redwood-pricing-tiers.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Emily Cho <emily@avahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: cost & benchmarking follow-up (token volumes + $/1M targets)

Ben —

Great recap, and thanks for the slide deck. Quick answers below to unblock the proposal team: 

Pilot volume estimates (rough):
- Pilot month 1: ~20M tokens (conservative)
- Pilot month 3: ~80M tokens (if pilot signals are positive)
- Steady-state month 6: ~150M tokens (our internal model for a single VA/region rollout)

Budget: Procurement gave a very rough band of $8k–$12k/month for the pilot (we need to see ROI to expand the budget materially). If throughput/latency are excellent we may reallocate.

Procurement: PO lead time is typically 2–4 weeks after commercial terms are agreed; they will need SOC2 Type II report + data handling appendix. I’ll loop procurement on the next message.

Do you have a target $/1M token you think fits that budget for a hosted pilot? We need a concrete figure to check against the band above.

Thanks —
Emily Cho
Head of Product
Ava Health

On Thu, Oct 29, 2026 at 03:12 PM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message omitted)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emily Cho <emily@avahealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@avahealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 19:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: cost & benchmarking follow-up (token volumes + $/1M targets)

Emily / Procurement —

Thanks for the numbers — that helps a lot. Quick, transparent proposal framing so you can validate against the $8k–$12k/month band. These are illustrative; we’ll finalize after procurement confirms constraints.

Short answer: for the pilot volumes you shared, we can run two viable pricing tracks: hosted pilot (fastest time to value) and dedicated-reserved pilot (lower unit cost but requires a short commit). Below are example tiers with benchmark comparisons vs common hosts (numbers shown are per 1M tokens and approximate for discussion):

Tier A — Hosted (no infra commitment, single-tenant logical isolation):
- Redwood hosted pilot price: $1.25 / 1M tokens (pilot tier)
- Comparable managed hosts (example): OpenAI-like managed endpoints: ~$1.60–$2.10 / 1M; Anthropic-like: ~$1.50–$2.00 / 1M
- Notes: Hosted gives fastest setup; includes prefix caching and basic KV caching that reduce effective tokens for many summary workflows.

Tier B — Dedicated / Reserved pool (1–3 month minimal reservation for pilot):

Subject: Tenant-led encryption and region commitments — follow-up

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. A few follow-ups from our security and platform teams as we evaluate the Private VPC option:

1) BYOK / key custody: we need clear confirmation that customer-managed keys (external KMS/HSM) can be used for disk encryption and snapshot encryption, and a brief outline of the key lifecycle on termination (revocation / scheduled rekey / cryptographic erase).
2) Geo-binding: AtlasCare requires that all persistent customer data and snapshots remain strictly in us-east-1 unless we authorize cross-region failover. What controls do you provide to lock a tenant to a single region and prevent automated replication?
3) Snapshot & backup deletion: when we decommission an environment or request a tenant wipe, what are the technical steps and expected timelines for logical deletion, snapshot purge, and physical media reclamation? Are there attestation artifacts you provide?
4) Evidence: we will need logs / signed attestation for the wipe event plus control-plane audit trail (timestamps, operator ids, file/object references).

Can you share an abbreviated runbook and an example attestation we can show our InfoSec folks? I've attached our residency questionnaire (atlas_residency_questions.pdf) for reference.

Appreciate a concise response we can present to legal & security. Also cc'ing our security team.

Thanks,
Leah Barnes
Director, Infrastructure Security
AtlasCare

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Leah Barnes <leah.barnes@atlascare.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-26T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Tenant-led encryption and region commitments — follow-up

Leah — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Karthik (security/compliance) and Marissa (deployments) to provide specifics and a short runbook.

High level answers up front:
- BYOK is supported for Private/VPC: we integrate with customer KMS (AWS KMS, external HSM via configured KMS proxy) and allow tenant keys to be used for volume and snapshot encryption.
- Region-lock: Private customers can enable a tenant-level geofence flag that prevents background replication or cross-region snapshot copying unless explicitly approved by both customer and Redwood.
- Deletion timelines: standard process is logical delete (control-plane tombstone) within 24–72 hours; cryptographic key revocation and proof-of-erase can be completed within 10 business days, physical reclaim timelines vary by provider but we provide attestation of deletion and hash-only evidence that data objects/snapshots were removed.

I'm attaching an options summary to start (redwood-residency-options.pdf). Karthik — can you produce the attestation template and a short examplar of the audit entries we can commit to? Marissa — please outline the operational steps for a tenant-led wipe and a 2-week pilot path.

Best,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--- attachments: redwood-residency-options.pdf ---

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Leah Barnes <leah.barnes@atlascare.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T11:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Tenant-led encryption and region commitments — follow-up

Hi Leah,

Thanks — below is a concise, technical Q&A and a minimal attestation workflow you can present to InfoSec.

1) BYOK / key custody details- Supported modes:
  • External KMS (customer-managed AWS KMS CMKs) — Redwood stores only key references; data keys are delivered to host agents via mTLS to the customer's KMS endpoint.
  • HSM proxy (for on-prem HSM) — we support an approved proxy pattern where key material never persists on-redwood.

2) Key lifecycle on termination- Steps we perform on formal decommission request:
  a) Freeze: disable new writes to the tenant volume (immediate).
  b) Snapshot tombstone: mark snapshots read-only and schedule purge workflow (within 24–72 hours).
  c) Key revocation: customer/Redwood revoke/deactivate the CMK or rotate to a zeroed key policy; once key is revoked, remaining ciphertext is inaccessible (cryptographic erase).
  d) Object purge: control-plane issues delete to object store; delete markers and manifest update are recorded.
  e) Attestation: we collect and sign audit records and provide revocation confirmation.

3) Geo-binding controls (how we prevent replication)-
  • Tenant-level geofence flag prevents any automated snapshot-copy or cross-region bucket replication.
  • Cross-region failover can only be enabled via a dual-approval workflow (customer API key + Redwood operator consent) with an auditable justification.

4) Snapshot & backup deletion timeline (expected)
  • T0: Decommission request acknowledged (within 4 hours).
  • T0+24–72h: Logical delete (snapshots tombstoned; access revoked).
  • T0+72h–T0+10 business days: Key revocation and evidence collection (signed audit bundle).
  • T0+30–90 days: Underlying provider reclamation complete (varies by object-store retention and vendor garbage collection); we provide a signed proof-of-delete package once all steps complete.

5) Evidence / attestation package- We can provide:
  • Signed control-plane audit bundle (timestamps, API calls, operator ids).
  • Snapshot manifest showing deleted snapshot IDs and delete markers.
  • KMS revocation record (redacted) showing key status changes.
  • SHA256 hashes of object manifests before and after deletion (to demonstrate removal without exposing content).

6) Pilot & verification approach (recommended)-
  • 2-week pilot where we deploy a dedicated tenant in us-east-1 with BYOK configured.
  • Run a controlled wipe at end of pilot to exercise the tombstone→revocation→attestation flow.
  • Deliver a signed attestation and the audit bundle for your audit team.

Operational notes / exceptions-
  • Snapshots in long-term cold storage or external backups (if you export them) are outside Redwood's automated purge; we will flag and document any externally managed exports.
  • Emergency cross-region replication for disaster recovery requires pre-approved SLOs and explicit consent; it will be logged and visible in the attestation.

I've attached a BYOK setup playbook and a sample attestation for reference. If you want, we can redact the production operator IDs in the sample for demo purposes.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer
Head, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

--- attachments: byok-setup-playbook.md, erase-attestation-sample.pdf ---

preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@medaxis.io>, Tom Brewer <tom.brewer@medaxis.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Hi Elena / Tom —

Thanks again for the deep dive last week. MedAxis is ready to move forward with the Dedicated deployment but needs a pricing structure that matches their constrained budget for H1. They asked for a one-off concession to make the pilot and regulatory work economical: 45% off list for an 18-month committed package (dedicated GPU pool + 24/7 support + VPC egress allowance) and onboarding fees waived.

High level:
- Expected baseline monthly spend: ~$120k list (tokens + seats + infra)
- Requested concession: 45% off list, onboarding waiver
- Term: 18 months, auto-renew unless flagged
- Strategic ask from MedAxis: compliance proofs and a private-control plane pilot (drives their timeline)

I'm asking for internal signoff from Sales leadership / Finance to route this to legal. Jordan, can you run a quick margin sensitivity and call out the downside at 45%? Marissa, flagging for VP/CFO signoff — they are pushing to close this quarter.

Attachments: preliminary quote (I'll attach the pricing sheet in the follow-up).

Next steps I propose:
1) Margin calc from Revenue Ops (Jordan) by EOD tomorrow
2) If economics are within tolerance, Marissa to escalate to Markus for CFO approval
3) I will send a tailored mutual action plan + draft addendum for legal

Would appreciate an expedited review — MedAxis expects an answer this week or they move to an alternate vendor.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Alex —

Thanks for the context. Quick read: MedAxis is strategic and the pilot has good reference potential, but a 45% flat concession for 18 months is large and will need CFO signoff. Jordan, please prioritize a one-page sensitivity that shows:
- Annualized ARR under list vs requested concession
- Gross margin impact (absolute $ and %) over 18 months
- Upside scenarios (usage overrun) and walkaway breakpoints

Also include proposed guardrails to limit downside (minimum commit floors, overage rates, performance milestones). If we can't quantify mitigations, I won't escalate a 45% concession.

Alex — think about a structured concession (stepped discount, usage-based rebate, or milestone unlocks) that preserves some margin while giving the customer the headline number. If you have a draft MAP or DocuSign template, include it in the follow-up.

I'll hold on escalation until Jordan sends the numbers. Markus, flagging you as the finance approver; happy to route to you for a decision once we have the sensitivity.

-Marissa
VP Sales, Commercial
Redwood Inference



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Marissa / Alex —

Attached are the worksheets: MedAxis_MA_Pricing_Sheet.xlsx and margin_sensitivity.csv. Summary below.

Summary (18-month view):
- List revenue (projected): $2,160,000
- Revenue @ 45% concession: $1,188,000 (delta -$972,000)
- Current gross margin (typical for this bundle): ~55%
- Projected gross margin @ 45% concession (no mitigations): ~28%
- Contribution margin drop: ~27 percentage points (~$263k reduction in gross profit over 18 months)

Mitigation scenarios modeled:
A) Stepped discount: 35% (months 1-6) -> 25% (months 7-18): lifts gross margin to ~40%
B) Usage floor + overage: Minimum commitment floor at 80% of expected tokens; overage at list rates above floor -> lifts margin to ~44% if utilization holds
C) Performance milestone unlock: additional 10% rebate contingent on achieving X API latency / uptime targets and 12-month consumption thresholds (transfers risk to customer)

My recommendation: Prefer A + B combined. That yields a tolerable margin loss and gives Salesteam the ability to market a headline discount while protecting downside.

Notes: all numbers assume current kernel costs and reserved GPU pricing; if hardware spot prices change, we should re-run.

-Jordan
Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment stubs: MedAxis_MA_Pricing_Sheet.xlsx (pricing tabs, cost model), margin_sensitivity.csv (scenario outputs)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@medaxis.io>, Tom Brewer <tom.brewer@medaxis.io>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Marissa / Jordan / Markus —

Thanks Jordan — useful sensitivity and very clear. Based on A + B (stepped discount + minimum floor) I discussed a compromise with MedAxis:
- Offer: 35% off list months 1-6, 25% off list months 7-18
- Minimum committed monthly spend floor at 80% of projected tokens (otherwise true-up)
- Onboarding fee waived conditional on DocuSign of MAP within 10 business days
- Performance unlock: if MedAxis exceeds 120% of projected usage for any 3-month rolling period, we offer a one-time credit (to avoid unlimited upside leakage)

I've uploaded a draft MAP and the proposed addendum here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1G-MedAxis-MAP (DocuSign link included in related_links).

If Sales Leadership + Finance can greenlight this banded concession, I'll send the legal-ready addendum and ask MedAxis to sign. Time is tight; they want an executable by 10/29.

Alex
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@azuresphere.ai>

Subject: Marketplace onboarding: AzureSphere - procurement intake

Hi Irene,

Thanks for the quick intro via the partner portal. We're ready to start the marketplace onboarding for AzureSphere's managed inference offering. To unblock listing and PO, I've attached our signed W-9 and the bank wiring instructions.

Attachments:
- AzureSphere_W9.pdf (application/pdf)
- AZS_wiring_instructions.pdf (application/pdf)

High-level ask: can Redwood accept a single monthly PO via the Azure Marketplace (transactable listing) or do we need a private offer/PO direct to Azure? Also, which procurement forms do you need from us for marketplace activation and for your finance team to route a PO?

Happy to hop on a short call this week — our procurement lead is cc'd.

Best,
Lisa Grant
Head of Finance
AzureSphere, Inc.
procurement@azuresphere.ai
+1 (415) 555-0132

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Grant <lisa.grant@azuresphere.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-13T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: AzureSphere - procurement intake

Hi Lisa — thanks for sending the W-9 and wiring stub. Quick answers up front: Redwood can support both a transactable Azure Marketplace listing and a private offer flow, but the PO routing differs and we'll need a couple of items from you to determine the cleanest path.

What we need from AzureSphere to move to intake and PO routing:
- Completed vendor intake form (doc attached) with exact legal entity name, EIN, registered address, and remittance details.
- SOC 2 Type II attestation (summary or link to report) or equivalent security evidence.
- Primary billing contact and email for invoicing and PO validation.
- Confirmation of desired marketplace path: transactable listing on Azure Marketplace or private offer via Azure CSP/partner channel.

If you choose a transactable Azure listing, Azure billing maps to buyer accounts via marketplace invoicing and Redwood will need the payor mapping details to set up billing. For a private offer, we typically accept a PO issued to Redwood with vendor payor mapping in our finance portal; that process requires a bit more paperwork but is straightforward.

I attached our vendor_intake_form.docx. Please return a completed copy plus any SOC 2/Security summary and your preferred go-live target.

If helpful, I can schedule a 30-minute intake call with Vanessa (marketplace ops) and Ben (finops) to walk through the PO routing.

Thanks,
Irene
Head, Marketplace Partnerships
Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>

title: HarborWave onboarding — SSO relaystate / invite link failing
preview:
From: Claire Tobin <ctobin@harborwave.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: HarborWave Procurement <procurement@harborwave.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: HarborWave: supplier portal invite link landing on 403 (relaystate missing)

Hi Ben —

We tried to accept the supplier portal invite you sent this morning but when our users click the link they land on a page showing:

   403 — RelayState parameter missing or invalid

This happens for users behind our corporate network and also from home. We've tested in Chrome (Incognito) and Safari. HarborWave is on Okta as the IdP; I shared a screenshot of the error (attached).

Can you reissue the invite or send the SP-initiated login URL? We need to get vendor setup completed to accept an upcoming PO (deadline end of week). Any quick workaround would be great.

Attachments: screenshot_relaystate.png

Thanks,
Claire Tobin
Procurement Manager | HarborWave
ctobin@harborwave.com | +1 415-555-0198



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Tobin <ctobin@harborwave.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HarborWave: supplier portal invite link landing on 403 (relaystate missing)

Claire — thanks for the screenshot, that error is consistent with a RelayState being removed by an intermediary (proxy/AMP) or an IdP-left flow expecting a different parameter. A couple quick things we can try:

- Confirm the invite was sent to the same email address that users sign into Okta with (sometimes subaddressing/case causes mismatch).
- Can you paste the full invite URL (or the portion after "/auth/redirect?")? That helps us detect if RelayState is present.
- Try the SP-initiated endpoint: https://portal.redwood.ai/sso/sp/harborwave/login — that bypasses the invite redirect and should kick a direct SAML flow.
- If possible, run a SAML trace or copy the Okta support case response (we attached simple instructions).

I'll also loop in Marissa from CS to offer a temporary deep-link if we confirm RelayState is stripped. I attached a short instr file for the Okta trace. If you can’t run the trace, we can generate a one-time tokened link for 72 hours as a stopgap.

Attachments: saml-trace-instructions.txt

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Customer Success Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai



From: Claire Tobin <ctobin@harborwave.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: HarborWave Procurement <procurement@harborwave.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HarborWave: supplier portal invite link landing on 403 (relaystate missing)

Thanks Ben — quick update after opening an Okta ticket. Okta's initial triage says the RelayState param is present on their side but appears to be removed by our network stack (we run a web gateway that rewrites URLs for inspection). They suggested using the SP-initiated URL you mentioned or WhiteList the full redirect URL.

We can't easily run the SAML trace for vendor users (company policy limits debug tools). I pasted the Okta reply below.

--- Okta support (excerpt):
"We see the request arriving without RelayState when forwarded through the inspection appliance. If you can provide a direct SP-initiated link or a tokened invite that does not rely on RelayState, that will allow users to complete the onboarding."

So yes, SP-initiated link would be the fastest. If you can also reissue an invite that uses a single-click acceptance (no intermediate redirect) that would be ideal.

On the timeline: HarborWave needs vendor profile completed and initial PO accepted by Friday COB. Can we escalate?

Claire

> Claire — thanks for the screenshot, that error is consistent with a RelayState being removed by an intermediary (proxy/AMP) or an IdP-left flow expecting a different parameter. A couple quick things we can try: ...

Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HarborWave: supplier portal invite link landing on 403 (relaystate missing)

Hi Claire — Marissa from Customer Success here, jumping in to offer a workaround while your network team coordinates with Okta.

Planned next steps (quick):
- I can generate a one-time, tokened deep-link that goes directly to the vendor onboarding flow (bypasses RelayState) and is valid for 72 hours.
- We'll also provision a temporary onboarding admin account so your procurement users can complete the profile and attach the PO.
- I'll include the PO template and a short checklist to speed completion.

Proposed call to finish handoff: Oct 24, 10:00 AM PT — I can join with an engineer if your team wants to run through the link live. Please confirm if that time works.

Attachments: PO-Template.xlsx

If that works I'll issue the deep-link and reply with the one-time URL (and TTL). Ben — please stand by to issue the link when I confirm.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

title: Privacy Safeguard Pack — deal structure and volume credits
preview:
From: Sara Ellison <sara@healthforge.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Privacy Safeguard Pack — initial ask

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. We liked the performance profile and the observability features — two things that really matter for our analytics pipelines. Before we proceed with the pilot, procurement asked for a clear pricing view on the set of security/compliance features we need. We're calling this the "Privacy Safeguard Pack" internally. Key asks:

- Audit logs retention: 7 years, exportable on request
- KMS-backed keys with HSM option for highest-risk buckets
- EU-only data residency (primary+backup in eu-west)
- HIPAA attestation for covered PHI processing in our use case

We have an anticipated 18-month rollout, starting with a pilot at ~50K generated tokens/day and scaling to ~2.5M tokens/day in month 12. Can you provide packaging and discounting options — e.g., standalone per-feature pricing, bundle price, and an annual committed option with credits? We also need to understand how credits/commitments would apply to dedicated vs hosted endpoints and whether KMS/HSM is charged as a seat, per-key, or as a managed add-on.

We're on a tight procurement window — hope to lock terms by end of next week. Attaching a short SOW sketch.

Best,
Sara
Sara Ellison
Head of Platform Partnerships
HealthForge Analytics
Attachment: HealthForge_SOW_draft.pdf (attached)


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Sara Ellison <sara@healthforge.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Privacy Safeguard Pack — initial ask

Hi Sara — thanks for the clear list. I'm looping in our pricing and security leads to map out options and give a workable quote. High-level notes up front:

- We can offer a modular approach (audit logs / KMS/HSM / residency / HIPAA add-on) or a bundled "Safeguard Pack" with a blended discount at commitment levels.
- KMS/HSM can be consumed as an add-on fee (managed HSM seat) plus a small per-request crypto op surcharge in hosted mode; Dedicated/Private customers can amortize HSM seats across instances.
- Residency and long retention are primarily driven by storage and retention policy costs — we can offer tiered credits for multi-region commitments.
- HIPAA attestation is an enterprise add-on (paperwork and assessment cycle) but we can include it in a package for an annual fee and evidence deliverables.

I'll come back with two concrete structures: 1) a pilot-focused month-to-month bundle with limited credits; 2) an annual committed model with volume discounts and a credits schedule.

Looping: Rishi (pricing), Lauren (security/product), Ben (sales lead).

—Chloe


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: (internal)
Date: 2026-10-21T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Privacy Safeguard Pack — initial ask

Chloe — quick internal thread with recommended structures. See attached pricing model spreadsheet (residency_pricing_model.xlsx).

Rishi (pricing) — suggested two tiers:
1) Pilot bundle (0–100K tokens/day): monthly billable bundle covering audit logs (90-day retention), KMS standard (managed keys, no HSM), EU residency replication; price = $3,000/mo + per-token usage. Pilot add-on for 7-year retention and HSM is available as one-off engineering request at $1,500/mo (temporary) or can be scoped into SOW.
2) Enterprise annual commit (>=12 months): Safeguard Pack bundled at 18% discount off list feature pricing at $0.14/token equivalent for compliance add-on (applied as per-token surcharge), plus HSM seats at $6,000/seat/year (capable of being amortized across Dedicated pools). Additional storage-retention fee of $0.02/GB-month for audit logs beyond default retention. Credits: 10% credits for >$150K ARR commit, 18% for >$400K.

Lauren (security/product) — notes on HIPAA & residency:
- HIPAA: We can provide attestation support and add the policies into the SOW. There is an onboarding audit that typically takes 3–5 weeks; price for attestation + compliance docs is $18,000 one-time or included as part of a multi-year contract with a $10,000 one-time reduced fee.
- Residency: We'll host primary+backup in eu-west and ensure backups are geo-fenced. For Dedicated installs in EU we need to confirm the customer's VPC/infra readiness; that'll affect implementation lead time and cost.

Ben (sales) — suggested commercial ask to customer: accept the Enterprise annual commit option if they can commit to a 12-month $200K ARR profile; offer a one-time pilot credit of $12,000 to offset initial migration and extended retention costs.

Action items: Chloe to combine these into two distinct offer letters (Pilot month-to-month; Annual committed Safeguard Pack) and share to Sara with SOW language.


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Sara Ellison <sara@healthforge.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T14:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Privacy Safeguard Pack — deal structures (pilot & annual commit)

Hi Sara — thanks for the patience, and apologies for the wall of internal detail. Two clear proposals below (high-level). I attached the SOW draft and pricing workbook.

Option A — Pilot (month-to-month)
- Duration: 3 months recommended; can be month-to-month
- Features: Audit logs (90-day default; long-retention available as paid add-on), KMS (managed keys, non-HSM), EU residency primary+backup
- Pricing: $3,000/mo base + usage ($0.00012/token generated for compliance surcharge)
- Pilot concession: One-time migration/retention credit = $12,000 applied to first invoice
- Add-ons: HSM (temporary for pilot) $1,500/mo or HSM seat $6,000/seat/year if requested
\Option B — Enterprise Annual Commit (recommended for production)
- Duration: 12 months minimum commit; 18–36 months available for deeper credits
- Features: Full Safeguard Pack (audit logs with 7-year retention tier, managed KMS + optional HSM seats, EU residency with backup geo-fencing, HIPAA attestation included)
- Pricing: Safeguard Pack surcharge equivalent to $0.00014/token (applied as per-token add-on) + HSM seats $6,000/seat/year + audit log storage $0.02/GB-month beyond baseline
- Credits: 10% account credit for >= $150K ARR; 18% for >= $400K ARR. Also offer accelerated implementation window (4 weeks vs typical 6–8) if contract signed within 10 business days.
\Next steps: If either option looks workable, we can produce an amended SOW. If you prefer, Ben can schedule a short call tomorrow to walk procurement through the economics.
\Best,
Chloe Martin
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Sent from my laptop
Attachment: residency_pricing_model.xlsx


From: Daniel Park <daniel@healthforge.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Ellison <sara@healthforge.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Privacy Safeguard Pack — deal structures (pilot & annual commit)

Chloe — appreciate the clarity and the pilot concession offer. We ran this on our side with procurement. A few notes:
- We'd like the pilot to include 7-year audit retention from day one (legal insists) — we understand the incremental storage fee; could you fold the first 12 months of retention storage into the migration credit instead of the separate $12K?
- For HSM, procurement prefers to buy 1 seat for the pilot but amortize it across Dedicated pools if we move to annual. Can you reflect a proration for year-1?
- We accept Option B in principle but need the 10% credit threshold to kick in at $125K (we're close); if you can do that, procurement will sign a 12-month commitment.

If you can update the SOW to: include 12 months of retention storage in the pilot credit, prorated HSM seat year-1 at $3,000, and the credit threshold at $125K, we'll route the doc for signature. Can Ben and Rishi mirror these changes in the workbook and send a revised SOW today? We have a DocuSign slot open this week.

Thanks — looking forward to closing.
Daniel Park
VP, Infrastructure & Security | HealthForge Analytics

title: Access ops kickoff: FinSecure SSO/SCIM and onboarding controls
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Frank Liu <frank.liu@finsecure.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Access ops kickoff: FinSecure SSO/SCIM and onboarding controls

Hi Frank / Priya,

Thanks for choosing Redwood — excited to get FinSecure live. I'm owning the onboarding coordination from the CS/sales side and looping in Laura (implementation lead) and Monica (customer success). Neha is on the security eval as a backup for policy questions.

Scope for this thread: get us SSO (SAML), SCIM provisioning, IP allowlist for outbound requests, and the initial service-account permissions & org policy expectations so we can schedule a staging test. Key asks below so we can hit a deployment window next week: 

1) SAML metadata: IdP metadata URL or metadata XML file (entityID, ACS, certificate).
2) SCIM admin: SCIM base URL and an admin token or OAuth client (we prefer a scoped token).

6) Compliance artifacts: if you have an internal SOC report or the contact for security questionnaire follow-up, share here.

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Frank Liu <frank.liu@finsecure.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Access ops kickoff: FinSecure SSO/SCIM and onboarding controls

Hi all — technical notes from the implementation side. We're aligned with Ben's checklist and will take the lead on the following items:

1) SCIM details we need from FinSecure:
   - SCIM base URL (full path, e.g., https://scim.finsecure.com/v2/)
   - Token scheme (Bearer) and whether token needs a prefix.
   - If using OAuth, please provide client_id and a transient client_secret (we will rotate secrets after provisioning).

2) SAML: we'll ingest the metadata XML. If FinSecure sends groups as an attribute, please confirm the attribute name (e.g., memberOf or groups) and whether it is a list of strings. Example mapping we recommend: 
   - groups -> Redwood role mapping: group_name=\"eng-admin\" -> app_role=admin; group_name=\"eng-user\" -> app_role=user

3) Service account: creating redwood-scim-bot@finsecure.com is preferred. Please ensure the account is set as non-interactive. Minimum permissions: Users:ReadWrite, Groups:ReadWrite. We do not need org-wide admin.

4) IP allowlist: if you can add a CIDR for Redwood's connectors that will simplify repeated changes. We'll send our connector CIDRs in the next email.

5) Staging test plan (we will perform the following in the 2–4pm window):
   - CREATE: create test user (scim-test-user+001@finsecure.com) and verify attributes (email, displayName).
   - GROUP SYNC: create a test group, add users, verify group membership mapping.
   - UPDATE: modify an attribute (title, manager) and confirm SCIM PATCH reflects change.
   - DEPROVISION: disable user and verify provisioning state and no access in SSO flow.

6) Rollback: if any provisioning call causes unexpected user lockouts, we will disable the connector and provide a remediation script to re-enable users.

Sample headers we will send for SCIM (for firewall rules):
   - Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>
   - Content-Type: application/scim+json

If FinSecure can provide a staging admin account (time-bound), that will avoid us needing to create test users in production. Otherwise we will perform all work in a dedicated sandbox org.

I'll attach a small sample mapping file and the staging runbook to the Drive link Ben included.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Implementation Lead, Redwood Inference

title: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC
preview:
From: Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: partner-success@clouddistrib.com
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC

Hi Chloe,

Hope you’re well. We have a time-sensitive POC opportunity with AzureSoft (procurement@azuredsoft.com) — they want to validate Redwood on a cross-region replica use case and have asked for trial credits that we can sponsor through our distributor program.

High-level ask:
- Total trial fund: $15,000 (usable over 3 months)
- Start: Nov 1 — End: Jan 31
- Guardrails: single-project scope, token caps, and expiring on end date

Can CloudDistrib route a partner-linked trial fund from Redwood or do you need a formal allocation? If yes, what paperwork do you need from us (SOW, distribution agreement, or a simple T&C)? We can also share a short mutual action plan and customer contact to speed approvals.

Attaching a one-pager of the customer use case.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call this week if helpful.

Best,
Maya
Partner Success | CloudDistrib
Attachments: AzureSoft_POC_onepager.pdf


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC

Hi Maya — thanks for the note and the one-pager.

We can support distributor-sponsored trial funds, but we usually need the allocation matrix and a brief T&C signoff so finance & legal can issue the credits. A few clarifying Qs to speed the review:

- Can CloudDistrib confirm the POC tenant/project ID (so we can hard-code usage caps)?
- Is AzureSoft open to a phased allocation (e.g., $5k upfront, $2k monthly top-ups) if we want to limit exposure?
- Any NDA/procurement constraints we should know about?

If it helps, Ben and Vivek are looped for partner ops and credit approvals. I can also propose a provisional allocation and a short DocuSign T&C to wrap up.

Proposed next step: Ben — can you draft a provisional allocation matrix we can send to Maya and AzureSoft?

-Chloe

On Sun, 18 Oct 2026 at 09:12, Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC

Thanks — reviewed the ask. We can provisionally approve a distributor-linked allocation, with the following constraints: 

- Approved amount: $12,000 total (provisionally reduced from $15k)
- Payout cadence: $6,000 upfront on activation, $3,000 at 30 days, $3,000 at 60 days (requires usage reports)
- Scope: single account/project (must provide project ID), token cap of 250k/month, automatic expiry on 2027-01-31
- Required docs: signed Trial Fund T&C (attached) and a short mutual action plan with milestones

I attached an allocation_matrix.xlsx with the payout schedule and a draft CloudDistrib_Trial_Fund_T&Cs.pdf. If AzureSoft wants additional runway we can consider a 1-month extension after a usage review.

Maya — can you confirm the project ID and loop in procurement at AzureSoft? If they sign the T&C we’ll route the credits and log the deal in HubSpot (deal_9421).

Best,
Ben
Attachments: allocation_matrix.xlsx, CloudDistrib_Trial_Fund_T&Cs.pdf


From: Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: partner-success@clouddistrib.com, procurement@azuredsoft.com
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 07:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC

Thanks Ben — appreciate the quick turnaround. AzureSoft procurement is OK with the amended $12k structure. Procurement contact is Lina Park (lina.park@azuredsoft.com) — she’s looped here.

AzureSoft asks one small change: a conditional one-month extension option if usage looks strong after the first 60 days (they won’t automatically draw it; it would be an addendum). Can Redwood approve a single optional extension (same token cap) pending the usage review you mentioned?

We’ll sign the T&C once you confirm the extension path and provide the DocuSign link.

-Maya


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya@clouddistrib.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@azuredsoft.com
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: distributor-linked trial fund for AzureSoft POC

Hi all — confirming final approval: we’ll proceed with the $12,000 distributor-linked allocation and include a one-time optional 30-day extension (subject to the usage review at day 60).

Next steps for Maya/AzureSoft:
1) Sign the attached CloudDistrib_Trial_Fund_T&Cs via DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/sign/XYZ-TRIAL-TC
2) Provide the Redwood project ID that will receive the credits
3) Ben/Chloe will schedule activation once DocuSign completes and we’ll paste the HubSpot deal link here (deal_9421)

Ben — please file the allocation in PartnerOps and confirm when the credits are loaded.

Thanks — Vivek
Senior Director, Partnerships | Redwood Inference

title: Management plane disruption during minor upgrade — remediation plan & next steps
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@alphamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 08:15:00 -0400
Subject: Management plane disruption during minor upgrade — remediation plan & next steps

Hi Kate —

I want to update you immediately about an incident that affected the management plane for your private VPC deployment during a minor control-plane agent upgrade we rolled this morning. Summary: a timing regression in our orchestration handshake (agent v2.3.1 -> v2.3.2) caused intermittent control-plane reconnect failures for a subset of nodes in the eu-west-1 region backing your environment.

What we did right away:
- Immediately paused the rollout and initiated an automated rollback to v2.3.1 for impacted pools (completed by 08:42 ET)
- Performed targeted restarts of the agent across affected nodes and validated connectivity (08:45 — 09:10 ET)
- Applied a temporary routing policy so traffic was routed to a compatible management proxy while the rollback completed

Impact to you: Some management-plane API calls (deploy, scale, config sync) returned errors for ~6–12 minutes during the window. Token-level request latency for control operations spiked but data plane inference remained available for the majority of requests—there was no user data exfiltration.

Immediate next steps we will take and timeline:
- Formal rollback confirmed and a full connectivity sweep completed by today EOD (we completed the sweep)
- Engineering postmortem & timeline to be shared by EOD Tuesday (attached: incident-timeline.pdf draft)
- Proposal for canary rollout policy changes and stricter preflight checks to depower this failure mode (proposal in JIRA INF-8792)
- We will issue a one-time service credit for the minutes of degraded control-plane availability; Marissa will follow up with specifics and a formal credit note

I know management-plane stability is critical for your SRE workflows; Ben is taking the lead on the remediation items and Laura is coordinating billing/credits. We’d like to schedule a short sync in the next 48 hours to walk through the root cause and proposed mitigations.

Apologies for the disruption — we take events like this seriously.

— Avery
Avery Johnson
Senior Customer Reliability Manager | Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: incident-timeline.pdf (draft)


From: Kate Miller <kate.miller@alphamed.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 08:37:12 -04:00
Subject: Re: Management plane disruption during minor upgrade — remediation plan & next steps

Thanks for the quick note, Avery. Appreciate the rollback and the early details. Two quick questions: 1) Any chance this exposed control-plane tokens or logs to external parties? 2) What will the credit look like (estimate minutes / dollar)?

We can do a 30-min sync tomorrow morning if that helps.

— Kate
Kate Miller
Head of Platform — AlphaMed Systems
kate.miller@alphamed.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:05:20 -04:00
Subject: FW: Management plane disruption — internal action items

Marissa / Avery — looping Eng and Ops in this thread. Quick notes from SRE triage:
- Root cause (preliminary): a race in the agent handshake when a new control-plane certificate was introduced during the upgrade; a path in the retry logic caused some nodes to enter a transient backoff that prevented timely reconnection.
- Mitigations completed: rollback, manual restarts, health-check promotion to prevent automatic rollout on similar signals.
- Short-term code fix: add deterministic backoff and a circuit-breaker on handshake retries (PR queued, target deploy to staging 10/19).

Avery — please confirm the customer sync time. Laura — please draft the credit note and attach the invoice delta for accounting.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Site Reliability | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@alphamed.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 11:22:05 -04:00
Subject: RE: Management plane disruption during minor upgrade — remediation plan & next steps

Hi Kate —

Quick responses to your questions: 1) No evidence of token or secrets exposure. Our audit logs show only internal agent-to-controlplane handshakes and no unusual downstream access patterns. We’ve preserved logs for the full window and will share the redacted log bundle for your security team. 2) Credit — we’ll calculate prorated service credit for the 12 minutes of confirmed degraded control-plane availability across your reserved nodes; Laura will send the formal credit amount and entry.

Attached is a concise incident timeline and an initial postmortem draft (see attachments). Our proposed immediate config changes for your environment:
- Move to a 3-step canary (1% -> 10% -> 100%) with a mandatory probe pass between steps
- Increase management plane health-check frequency for control sync to catch regression spikes
- Add a safety circuit: if handshake retry rate > X for 2 mins, roll back automatically

We recommend a 30-min call Tuesday 10/20 at 09:00 ET to walk the RCA and answer any technical questions. Would that work for your team?

Thanks —
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
Attachments: incident-timeline.pdf, execution-log.txt


From: Kate Miller <kate.miller@alphamed.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:05:40 -04:00
Subject: Re: Management plane disruption during minor upgrade — remediation plan & next steps

Thanks, Marissa — that allay concerns. 09:00 ET on Tuesday is good for us. A couple asks for the postmortem: please include the exact hashes of agent binaries rolled during the window, a redacted copy of the handshake logs, and the intended SLA credit calculation so we can review with finance. Also please include the change to canary policy as config snippets we can apply on our end.

We look forward to the call.

— Kate


From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@alphamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2026 16:30:00 -04:00
Subject: Postmortem & next steps — management plane disruption (RCA summary)

Hi Kate and team —

Shared below is a concise RCA and the immediate remediation plan. Full postmortem & artifacts are in the Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1abcDEFgHIj (link above) and ticketed as JIRA INF-8792. Attachments include the final incident timeline and a postmortem draft for your review.

RCA (concise):
- Trigger: minor agent upgrade introduced a certificate rotation path that, when combined with a narrow timing window in our agent handshake, produced a deterministic but rare race.
- Consequence: transient handshake failures that placed ~8 nodes into a retry backoff, causing control-plane operation errors for approximately 12 minutes (window confirmed).
- Data safety: no evidence of keys/tokens exported or abnormal data-plane traffic — logs retained and redacted bundle available for your SecOps.

Remediation & guardrails being implemented:
- Code: deterministic retry backoff + circuit-breaker on handshake (deploy to staging 10/19, canary 10/21)
- Process: enhanced preflight checks for certificate rotation scenarios and an expanded canary progression (1%/10%/100%)
- Ops: improved alerting thresholds and an automated rollback trigger for high handshake-failure rates
- Customer-runbook: we will provide config snippets for your environment and a short runbook for emergency manual rollback steps

Credits & next steps:
- Laura will issue the formal prorated credit by EOD Monday and include line items in your next invoice.
- We’ve scheduled the follow-up call for Tue 10/20 09:00 ET (calendar invite sent).

We apologize again for the disruption. Ben and Marissa are available on the call to walk through the timeline and technical details. If useful, we can bring in the engineering lead who authored the handshake change.

Regards,
Avery Johnson
Senior Customer Reliability Manager | Redwood Inference
Attachments: postmortem-draft.md

title: Skyline: API change notice — impact heatmap & migration proposal
preview:
From: Rajiv Kapoor <rajiv@skyline.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync and new streaming header

Hi Ben,

Quick heads-up from the Skyline platform team: we're planning to remove our legacy /v1/sync endpoint in favor of a streaming-first API surface that uses the X-Skyline-Stream header and a chunked responses model. Target rollout for partner API consumers is the week of 2026-11-29, with a hard cut 2027-01-15.

We know Redwood integrations rely on the sync behavior for batch uplift at ingest; we ran a quick impact scan and attached a short spec and a preliminary impact matrix. Can you share who on your side we should loop in for technical alignment and to coordinate a migration/workshop?

Key notes:
- New header: X-Skyline-Stream: true (streaming responses)
- Legacy endpoint will continue returning status 200 until cut date, but will stop accepting new API keys after 2026-12-15
- No change to auth scheme beyond key lifecycle, but response framing and retry behavior will differ

Attachments: skyline-change-spec.pdf, partner-impact-heatmap.csv (stubbed)

Thanks,
Rajiv Kapoor
Product Manager — Integrations
Skyline Analytics
[attachment: skyline-change-spec.pdf]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Kapoor <rajiv@skyline.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@skyline.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T12:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync and new streaming header

Rajiv / Elena — thanks for the flag and the attachments.

We definitely want to avoid any break for our shared customers. First pass from my side:
- I'll set up a short migration workshop with our Partner Engineering team next week.
- Can you confirm whether the streaming model will expose partial result tokens for long-run queries (i.e., partial doc embeddings) or whether it will only change framing for batches?
- We also need a sample of the new response envelope and error semantics so our Redwood runtime team can evaluate fallback and buffering options.

I'll loop in Vanessa (Alliances) and recommend we bring Marissa (Runtime PM) and Jonas (Integrations lead) into the thread for timeline/rollout discussion. Would a migration window of 8–10 weeks be acceptable to Skyline? If you can keep legacy keys active until 2026-12-31 for partners who opt into the migration plan, we can propose a staged rollout.

Proposed next steps:
1) 30-min scoping call (this week) — confirm response envelope and error semantics
2) Migration workshop + sample tests (by 2026-10-28)
3) Canary plan + customer list for staged cutover (Nov–Dec)

I attached a one-page offer we normally share for partner migrations (rw-migration-offer.pdf).

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
[attachment: rw-migration-offer.pdf]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync (Skyline) — internal impact + ask

Team — forwarding Skyline's notice. TL;DR: partner will remove legacy /v1/sync and move to streaming header X-Skyline-Stream. They propose cut 2027-01-15; keys frozen 2026-12-15. They attached an impact heatmap.

What I need from you:
- Marissa: quick assessment on runtime changes (buffering, KV cache implications, streaming tokenization). Estimated effort?
- Jonas: integration testing plan + how quickly can we build a compatibility shim for our hosted API to emulate sync semantics over streaming?
- Vanessa: alliance asks — are we offering migration credits or prioritized engineering slots for Skyline customers? Ben suggested staged cutover and an 8–10 week migration window.

We're proposing to ask Skyline to keep legacy keys active until 2026-12-31 if we commit to a canary plan and customer list.

Forwarded message below.

--
Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T11:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync (Skyline) — runtime assessment

Ben / team — quick tech note after scanning the spec:

Summary of impact (runtime):
- Streaming responses will require changes in our continuous-batching layer: currently we assume a final response envelope for tokenization and embedding aggregation. We'll need to support incremental token flush and finalization hooks.
- KV cache behavior: streaming partials mean we must persist partial state per request until final frame. This increases memory retention per connection; worst-case memory bump estimated ~12–18% for long-tail requests.
- Compatibility shim: we can add a server-side shim that buffers streaming chunks and emits a synchronous response to callers, but it adds latency (typical extra 80–250ms depending on token count) and moderate CPU overhead.

Estimated scope & timeline:
- Prototype shim and test harness: 2 engineer-weeks (1 eng)
- Load testing and perf tuning: 2 engineer-weeks (1 eng)
- Canary rollout and monitoring hooks: 1 engineer-week (0.5 eng)

If Skyline can keep legacy keys active until 2026-12-31 that gives us a 10-week window to prototype, test, and canary. With that, I recommend we offer a staged migration where we:
1) Build shim + run compatibility tests against Skyline's sample payloads
2) Offer 1–2 top customers a prioritized migration (migration credits + dedicated support)
3) After successful canaries, flip default to streaming and retire shim after 30 days of stable telemetry

I'll attach a quick heatmap (partner-impact-heatmap.csv) and a proposed monitoring checklist.

-Marissa
Principal PM, Runtime
Redwood Inference
[attachment: partner-impact-heatmap.csv]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Kapoor <rajiv@skyline.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@skyline.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming API change: impact heatmap & migration workshop — proposed plan and next steps

Rajiv / Elena — thanks for the quick heads-up and for the spec. We scoped internally and can support a migration with the following offer: 

Proposed Redwood migration plan for Skyline partners:
- Keep legacy keys writable for partners enrolled in the migration program until 2026-12-31 (Skyline consent requested)
- Redwood builds a compatibility shim (buffers streaming chunks, emits sync-style envelopes) with an expected perf penalty of ~80–250ms; prototype + canary in ~5 engineer-weeks
- Prioritized migration slots for up to 3 co-managed customers (includes 2 weeks of engineering support and migration credits as detailed in rw-migration-offer.pdf)
- Monitoring & rollback hooks: 30-day post-cut monitoring; shim will be used as fallback for first 14 days after flip

Next steps:
1) Confirm Skyline will keep legacy keys writable for enrolled partners until 2026-12-31 and provide sample streaming payloads and error cases by 2026-10-25
2) 30-min scoping call: propose 2026-10-24 at 10:00 PT (I'll send a calendar invite)
3) Migration workshop (60–90 mins) week of 2026-10-26 — agenda attached (migration_workshop-agenda.docx)

If that works for you we'll open a short HubSpot play (DL-8372) and assign an internal SLA for the co-managed customers.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
[attachments: rw-migration-offer.pdf, migration_workshop-agenda.docx]
