Date: 2026-10-19T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync (Skyline) — internal impact + ask

Team — forwarding Skyline's notice. TL;DR: partner will remove legacy /v1/sync and move to streaming header X-Skyline-Stream. They propose cut 2027-01-15; keys frozen 2026-12-15. They attached an impact heatmap.

What I need from you:
- Marissa: quick assessment on runtime changes (buffering, KV cache implications, streaming tokenization). Estimated effort?
- Jonas: integration testing plan + how quickly can we build a compatibility shim for our hosted API to emulate sync semantics over streaming?
- Vanessa: alliance asks — are we offering migration credits or prioritized engineering slots for Skyline customers? Ben suggested staged cutover and an 8–10 week migration window.

We're proposing to ask Skyline to keep legacy keys active until 2026-12-31 if we commit to a canary plan and customer list.

Forwarded message below.

--
Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T11:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync (Skyline) — runtime assessment

Ben / team — quick tech note after scanning the spec:

Summary of impact (runtime):
- Streaming responses will require changes in our continuous-batching layer: currently we assume a final response envelope for tokenization and embedding aggregation. We'll need to support incremental token flush and finalization hooks.
- KV cache behavior: streaming partials mean we must persist partial state per request until final frame. This increases memory retention per connection; worst-case memory bump estimated ~12–18% for long-tail requests.
- Compatibility shim: we can add a server-side shim that buffers streaming chunks and emits a synchronous response to callers, but it adds latency (typical extra 80–250ms depending on token count) and moderate CPU overhead.

Estimated scope & timeline:
- Prototype shim and test harness: 2 engineer-weeks (1 eng)
- Load testing and perf tuning: 2 engineer-weeks (1 eng)
- Canary rollout and monitoring hooks: 1 engineer-week (0.5 eng)

If Skyline can keep legacy keys active until 2026-12-31 that gives us a 10-week window to prototype, test, and canary. With that, I recommend we offer a staged migration where we:
1) Build shim + run compatibility tests against Skyline's sample payloads
2) Offer 1–2 top customers a prioritized migration (migration credits + dedicated support)
3) After successful canaries, flip default to streaming and retire shim after 30 days of stable telemetry

I'll attach a quick heatmap (partner-impact-heatmap.csv) and a proposed monitoring checklist.

-Marissa
Principal PM, Runtime
Redwood Inference
[attachment: partner-impact-heatmap.csv]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Kapoor <rajiv@skyline.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@skyline.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming API change: impact heatmap & migration workshop — proposed plan and next steps

Rajiv / Elena — thanks for the quick heads-up and for the spec. We scoped internally and can support a migration with the following offer: 

Proposed Redwood migration plan for Skyline partners:
- Keep legacy keys writable for partners enrolled in the migration program until 2026-12-31 (Skyline consent requested)
- Redwood builds a compatibility shim (buffers streaming chunks, emits sync-style envelopes) with an expected perf penalty of ~80–250ms; prototype + canary in ~5 engineer-weeks
- Prioritized migration slots for up to 3 co-managed customers (includes 2 weeks of engineering support and migration credits as detailed in rw-migration-offer.pdf)
- Monitoring & rollback hooks: 30-day post-cut monitoring; shim will be used as fallback for first 14 days after flip

Next steps:
1) Confirm Skyline will keep legacy keys writable for enrolled partners until 2026-12-31 and provide sample streaming payloads and error cases by 2026-10-25
2) 30-min scoping call: propose 2026-10-24 at 10:00 PT (I'll send a calendar invite)
3) Migration workshop (60–90 mins) week of 2026-10-26 — agenda attached (migration_workshop-agenda.docx)

If that works for you we'll open a short HubSpot play (DL-8372) and assign an internal SLA for the co-managed customers.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
[attachments: rw-migration-offer.pdf, migration_workshop-agenda.docx]

Hi Alicia — great conversation earlier. Thanks for sharing more about StackBridge's ingestion pipeline and the latency targets you need for the search assistant. Quick recap and suggested next steps below so we keep momentum: 

- What we heard: your priority is sub-200ms token latency for interactive search, tight budget for inference spend in Q4, and strong auditability for PII redaction.
- Who should attend next: your engineering lead (tech deep dive) + one product manager to align on success criteria.
- Goal of next meeting: 30-minute product intro focused on fit + a short tech checklist so Soojin can advise on model/quantization choices.

Can you confirm the right two stakeholders on your side? If so, please pick one of the time windows below (all times PT):

Option A — Wed Oct 27, 9:00–9:30am PT (good for West Coast engineering)
Option B — Thu Oct 28, 11:30–12:00pm PT (midday, better for product folks on East Coast)
Option C — Fri Oct 29, 4:00–4:30pm PT (short block for exec-aligned overview)

If none of these work, share two 30-minute windows and we’ll do our best to pair calendars. I’m copying Soojin (technical lead) and Priyom (CS) so they can jump in on timing and any pre-demo asks. I’ve attached the discovery notes and our short one-pager for context.

Next steps if you pick a slot: I’ll send a calendar invite with a short agenda and a prep checklist for the tech call (sample prompts, dataset pointers, and a short security note).

title: Integration manifest: HyperCart evaluation & SDK plan
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Alex Geller <alex.geller@hypercart.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@hypercart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Integration manifest: HyperCart evaluation & SDK plan

Hi Alex / Priya — great demo earlier. Quick recap and a proposed "integration manifest" to keep the POC moving:

Summary (one line): Redwood will provide SDK + streaming API routes, a tool-calling surface, and an acceptance checklist so HyperCart can validate cost, latency, and accuracy for the checkout-assistant use case.

Draft success criteria (please confirm):
- Accuracy: >= 92% intent-match on the two sample intents we ran today (abbrev: intent-match).
- Latency: 95th-percentile response < 400ms for single-turn chat on our Dedicated profile.
- Throughput: sustain 150 reqs/sec for batched inference with <= 15% tail-latency uplift.
- Cost: projected token cost <= $0.0025/token for production traffic profile (see attached estimate).

Immediate next steps and owners:
- Camila (me): finalize contract terms and timeline for POC kickoff.
- Ben Carter: technical implementation guide, SDK routes, and example streaming snippet (due: Wed 10/21).
- Marissa Cole: success criteria validation plan and acceptance checklist.
- HyperCart: confirm tech owner(s) for engineering staging environment and dataset access.

Attachments: MVP_traffic_estimate_Q4.csv (traffic profile we used), integration_manifest_v1.pdf (details below + diagrams).

Can you confirm the success criteria above and the tech owner on your side? If you’re aligned I’ll ask Ben to drop a short implementation manifest with endpoints and a proposed 3-week timeline.

Thanks — excited to get this moving.

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192



From: Alex Geller <alex.geller@hypercart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@hypercart.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Integration manifest: HyperCart evaluation & SDK plan

Thanks Camila — that summary is helpful. A few clarifications from our side before we lock the manifest:

1) Success criteria: the accuracy metric is fine, but can you add a throughput validation step that includes simulated concurrent shopping sessions (we expect spiky traffic around flash sales).
2) Data residency: HyperCart requires EU data residency for EU customer traffic — will the Dedicated profile allow region routing during the POC?
3) Tool-calling: we use a lightweight product catalog lookup service that the assistant must call for entity resolution. Please include any constraints on synchronous tool-calling latency and retry semantics.
4) API routes: we’d like a minimal list of SDK routes (chat, embeddings, rerank) and a streaming example (we prefer server-sent events or websockets).

Also: can you assign an on-call technical owner on Redwood for the first week of the POC? We want someone who can hop on a call if we see integration errors.

Appreciate it — happy to sync quickly tomorrow if that’s easier.

—
Alex Geller
Head of Integrations, HyperCart
alex.geller@hypercart.com



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Alex Geller <alex.geller@hypercart.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@hypercart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Integration manifest: HyperCart evaluation & SDK plan

On Tue, Oct 20, 2026 at 9:10 AM Alex Geller <alex.geller@hypercart.com> wrote:
> Thanks Camila — that summary is helpful. A few clarifications from our side before we lock the manifest:
>
> 1) Success criteria: the accuracy metric is fine, but can you add a throughput validation step that includes simulated concurrent shopping sessions (we expect spiky traffic around flash sales).
> 2) Data residency: HyperCart requires EU data residency for EU customer traffic — will the Dedicated profile allow region routing during the POC?
> 3) Tool-calling: we use a lightweight product catalog lookup service that the assistant must call for entity resolution. Please include any constraints on synchronous tool-calling latency and retry semantics.
> 4) API routes: we’d like a minimal list of SDK routes (chat, embeddings, rerank) and a streaming example (we prefer server-sent events or websockets).

Thanks — good points. Below is the technical manifest Ben put together (condensed). Full PDF is attached.

INTEGRATION MANIFEST (condensed)

1) SDK / API surface (minimal):
- POST /v1/chat: standard chat calls, supports messages, system prompts, and function/tool calling.
- POST /v1/embeddings: vectorization for product search and reranking.
- POST /v1/rerank: candidate reranking using embeddings + context.
- Websocket /v1/stream/chat: bidirectional streaming for low-latency interactive flows (fallback to REST streaming).

Examples: cURL + JS snippet included in attached PDF (integration_manifest_v1.pdf).

2) Streaming plan:
- Preferred: websocket with binary-framed tokens + JSON control messages.
- Alternative: server-sent events (SSE) when websocket is blocked.
- KV cache warmup enabled for repeated product lookups to reduce cold-start latency.

3) Tool-calling / function protocol:
- Redwood supports function call spec (name + typed schema).
- Synchronous tool-calls: recommended timeout 300ms; retries: exponential backoff up to 2 retries.
- If the product-catalog lookup exceeds 300ms, Redwood will return a structured fallback intent to avoid blocking the chat flow.

4) Auth & environment:
- API keys scoped to a test environment. For POC we will issue staging keys with restricted scopes.
- EU traffic routing: For EU-origin requests we will route to our Frankfurt Dedicated nodes (EU residency) — confirmed for POC.

5) Owners & on-call:
- Primary tech owner (implementation): Ben Carter (ben.carter@redwood.com). Ben will be on-call the first week and join troubleshooting calls.
- Success criteria & acceptance testing: Marissa Cole (marissa.cole@redwood.com).
- Commercial / contracting: Camila Reyes.

6) Timeline (proposed, calendar days):
- Day 0 (kickoff): sign manifest and share staging credentials.
- Days 1–3: sandbox wiring, auth test, simple chat smoke tests.
- Days 4–10: integration of product-catalog tool-calls + streaming verification.
- Days 11–17: load/throughput validation (simulated flash sale tests) and regression checks against provided eval prompts.
- Day 18–21: acceptance window; sign-off or defined remediation plan.

Acceptance tests mapped to success criteria: accuracy tests run daily against the sample dataset; throughput run with 30m spike tests to validate 150 reqs/sec target. Detailed test plan in attached PDF.

Attachments referenced: integration_manifest_v1.pdf (contains full cURL examples, JS websocket example, and diagram of routing rules).

Next step: If you confirm the timeline and owners I’ll schedule the kickoff for Monday 10/25 and ask Ben to drop the SDK snippets in a shared Drive folder.

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference



From: Alex Geller <alex.geller@hypercart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@hypercart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Integration manifest: HyperCart evaluation & SDK plan

Camila / team — this looks good and thanks for the detail. We confirm:

- Success criteria: agreed pending minor wording on throughput test (we'll send a 1-page spike profile tomorrow).
- EU routing: confirmed, thanks for routing to Frankfurt nodes for EU-origin traffic.
- Tech owner: our lead integration engineer will be Priya Shah (priya.shah@hypercart.com). She’ll coordinate on staging credentials and schedule.
- Kickoff: Monday 10/25 works for us — please send a calendar invite.

One ask before kickoff: can Ben include a short trace-level logging example so we can map request IDs from our side to Redwood logs during the spike tests? That will help speed debug loops.

Appreciate it — see you Monday.

—
Alex Geller
Head of Integrations, HyperCart

title: Contract endgame: timeline & notice owner assignments
preview:
From: Laura Emmerson <laura.emerson@skybridgehealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Skybridge Procurement <procurement@skybridgehealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Skybridge MSA — term language and notice windows (redline attached)

Hi Avery,

Thanks for taking this on. Attached is our procurement redline (Skybridge_MSA_redline_v3.docx) — the main open items for us right now are:

- Auto-renew mechanics and whether renewals are by invoice or by execution of order forms.
- Notice windows for non-renewal (we've suggested 90 days in our redline).
- Termination for convenience (we prefer a 60-day notice with a pro-rated refund for unused committed capacity).

Can you confirm Redwood's preferred baseline and whether you can accept our 90-day opt-out window? If not, please propose a compromise. We need an internal timeline to complete legal review + procurement signoff by Nov 12 to keep our internal renewal calendar intact.

Attaching the redline and a procurement checklist. Happy to jump on a quick call if easier.

Thanks,
Laura
Laura Emmerson
Senior Director, Procurement
Skybridge Health
Attachment: Skybridge_MSA_redline_v3.docx (document stub)
Attachment: Procurement_SLA_Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Emmerson <laura.emerson@skybridgehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Skybridge MSA — term language and notice windows (redline attached)

Laura — thanks for the clear note and the redline. Quick responses up front:

- Baseline: Redwood generally prefers an auto-renew by invoice for order forms tied to committed capacity, with an opt-out notice window rather than manual re-sign. That gives customers continuity and avoids disruption.
- Notice windows: 90 days is higher than our standard for mid-market pilots, but reasonable for larger committed deals — we typically propose 60 days for standard commitments and 90 days for enterprise-long commitments.
- Termination for convenience: we can agree in principle to a TFC clause but need to protect committed capacity pricing/notice by including a pro-rated refund/settlement for unused prepayments.

Marissa — can you weigh in on the legal tradeoffs (90 vs 60) and whether we can accept the 60-day TFC language they propose with the refund mechanics? Ben — if we need a pricing concession to move to 90 days, can you estimate the unit impact so we can present it as a concession?

Proposed timeline to keep Skybridge's date:
- Today: confirm negotiation leads & owners (Avery, Marissa, Ben)
- Wed 10/23: 30-min call to align on redline positions (I'll set)
- Mon 10/28: Redwood sends counter-redline PDF (RDW_MSA_redline_comments.pdf)
- Nov 2–5: 2nd pass with Skybridge legal, finalize language
- Nov 9–12: Exec signoff and DocuSign routing

If that works I'll schedule Wed 11:00 PT. I attached our counter comments PDF for your review.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Lead, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
Attachment: RDW_MSA_redline_comments.pdf (PDF stub)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Emmerson <laura.emerson@skybridgehealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Skybridge MSA — term language and notice windows (redline attached)

Looping in legal commentary inline — Laura, thanks for the redline. A few specific points from our side:

1) Notice window compromise
- Suggested approach: tiered notice window written into the order form:
  * Pilot / Trial: 30 days
  * Standard committed order (<=12 months): 60 days
  * Multi-year / enterprise (>12 months or >$500k TCV): 90 days
- Rationale: preserves customer flexibility while protecting our capacity planning and pricing.

2) Termination for Convenience (TFC)
- We can accept a TFC right but request the following guardrails:
  * TFC requires 60 days' prior written notice.
  * Customer is responsible for payments for the remainder of the then-current paid term unless we agree to a pro-rated refund for pre-paid amounts.
  * If TFC is exercised within the first 30 days of a committed term, we propose a 30% early-termination fee to cover provisioning costs.

3) Auto-renew mechanics
- Prefer auto-renew via invoice unless customer elects manual renewal in the order form. We can offer a manual renew opt-in for select enterprise customers at negotiation.

Sample snippet we can include in a counter-redline (feel free to copy into their doc):
"""
Order Form Term and Renewal: Unless otherwise stated in the applicable Order Form, the Initial Term shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for the period set forth therein. Each Order Form will automatically renew for successive renewal terms equal to the expiring term unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal in accordance with the notice periods set forth in the Order Form.
"""

Next steps: I'll draft the counter-snippet and share it by EOD Monday. Ben — if you can provide an estimated dollar impact for moving our standard 60-day to 90-day for this deal, include it in the counter.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Commercial
Redwood Inference

On Wed, Oct 21, 2026 at 10:03 AM Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Laura — thanks for the clear note and the redline.....


From: Laura Emmerson <laura.emerson@skybridgehealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Skybridge Procurement <procurement@skybridgehealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T12:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Skybridge MSA — term language and notice windows (redline attached)

Marissa, Avery — appreciate the quick turnaround. A couple clarifications from Procurement:

- Tiered notice windows are acceptable as long as they are explicit in the order form; we will flag any exceptions to our governance board but the 90-day ask is mostly for multi-year deals.
- For TFC, Skybridge prefers that the refund be automatic and pro-rated for unused prepaid months (we want to avoid case-by-case negotiations post-termination). Can you propose exact pro-rata math so procurement can model cash flow?

Also, one ask: given our compliance rhythm, would Redwood consider an alternative auto-renew default where the order form auto-renews unless Customer provides notice no later than 30 days prior to renewal? (That reduces administrative friction on our side.)

If you can send language for the pro-rata refund and the alternate 30-day opt-out phrasing, we'll review and confirm whether Procurement needs board escalation.

Thanks — happy to keep the Wed 11:00 PT call if you want to walk through items.

Laura
Attachment: Procurement_SLA_Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Emmerson <laura.emerson@skybridgehealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T15:38:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Skybridge MSA — term language and notice windows (redline attached)

- Marissa: own the legal redline and produce two concrete snippets by EOD Monday — (A) tiered notice table for order form, (B) pro-rata refund math and suggested clause for automatic pro-rated refunds.
- Ben: run pricing impact for moving a typical 12-month committed order from 60->90 days (ballpark impact and recommended concession).
- Me (Avery): schedule the 30-min alignment call with Skybridge for Wed 11:00 PT and own the meeting invite / agenda; I'll include HubSpot deal record and the DocuSign prep link.

I'll send an invite in the next 20 minutes. Laura — thanks again; we'll come back with the two language options (60/90) and precise refund language for your procurement model.

Avery Johnson
Account Lead, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

- Me (Avery): schedule the 30-min alignment call with Skybridge for Wed 11:00 PT and own the meeting invite / agenda; I'll include HubSpot deal record and the DocuSign prep link.

I'll send an invite in the next 20 minutes. Laura — thanks again; we'll come back with the two language options (60/90) and precise refund language for your procurement model.

Avery Johnson
Account Lead, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

title: Intermediary seller chain-of-custody (CoC) & resale certificate — invoice hold
preview:
From: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Invoice 847 hold — missing intermediary chain-of-custody

Hi Aisha,

Quick note from Marketplace billing — we received Redwood invoice #847 for Marketplace Inc (remit to: Gina Lopez / Wholesale Co.) but the buyer (intermediary seller ID 9987) provided a resale certificate covering Sales Tax only at the end-customer level. Our compliance team is requesting chain-of-custody documentation that ties the reseller (the marketplace seller) to the shipped goods / billed entity.

Specifically they're asking for one of the following:
- a signed POA/letter from the reseller authorizing Marketplace to act as the seller of record and confirming resale coverage,
- a ledger export showing the intermediary seller->end-customer flow for this SKU (invoice range / dates), or
- a notarized resale certificate explicitly listing the intermediary seller entity and the Marketplace as facilitating entity.

Until we receive acceptable CoC evidence, Marketplace has placed the invoice on hold for tax clearance and final payment. Can you confirm whether Redwood can supply any of the above or if Marketplace will accept a digitally signed affidavit from the seller?

Thanks,
Marketplace Ops
[Marketplace Inc — Billing]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice 847 hold — missing intermediary chain-of-custody

Thanks for the heads-up — looping in revenue ops and the account owner to triage.

Ben / Tessa — Marketplace is asking for chain-of-custody documentation for invoice 847 tied to intermediary seller ID 9987. Invoice details: PO# MP-847, Gross USD 42,300, shipped 2026-09-28. The seller (Gina Lopez / Wholesale Co.) submitted a state resale certificate that covers retail sales, but it does not name Marketplace or show transfer of title.

Current ask: can Redwood provide any of the three items Marketplace listed? If not, do we have authority to request a signed affidavit from Wholesale Co. that covers the transaction and names Marketplace as the facilitating seller? This is blocking the payment — accounting flagged it as a payment collection risk.

If we can get a ledger export (seller-side) mapping the sale to the end-customer, that would be fastest. If the seller will only provide a certificate that names their own EIN, Marketplace may still reject it.

Next steps I propose:
- Ben to contact Wholesale Co. procurement (Gina) to request a signed POA or ledger export.
- Tessa to prepare a standard Redwood affidavit template Redwood can share if seller prefers digital attestation.
- Karthik to advise on tax acceptance policy (multi-state) and whether a redline is acceptable.

I'll follow up tomorrow with an update.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive — Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
[aisha_rahman@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0132]

title: High-touch deal: feature accelerant & roadmap gating
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elaine Torres <elaine.torres@novacorp.com>, Procurement <procurement@novacorp.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: High-touch deal: feature accelerant & roadmap gating

Team — quick context and ask. NovaCorp Financial (large regional bank) is at the final commercial review for a ~$1.2M annual committed contract. Procurement has a gating item in their SOC/compliance review: they want per-tenant SLA tagging and a small telemetry tag in the trace (metadata key) so their ops can route incidents to their on-call runbook.

Why this matters now:
- They will sign this quarter if we can commit to delivery within their activation window (target go-live: 2026-12-15).
- They asked that this be available as a scoped flag in our Dedicated config (not a full product change).
- Without it, they are likely to push to competitive buyers with on-prem deployments.

Concrete ask to Product/Eng:
1) Can we scope a minimal per-tenant SLA tagging feature (expose tenant_sla_tag in Dedicated tenant config + add lightweight trace metadata kv) that can be shipped behind a feature flag?
2) What is the earliest realistic timeline for a canary release to a single tenant in Dedicated? Please include E2E QA and rollout steps.
3) If full change is >6 weeks, what's the minimal workaround we can offer that still satisfies procurement (e.g., a contract-side runbook + observability playbook + temporary manual mapping)?

I've attached the SOW-draft-v2 and a short traffic-matrix showing expected percent of traffic we expect to route to the Dedicated pool.

This is time-sensitive — polite ask for an initial triage reply today and a quick sync tomorrow at 10am PT if helpful. I can own customer comms.

Thanks,
Ben
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: SOW-draft-v2.docx)


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High-touch deal: feature accelerant & roadmap gating

Ben — thanks for the clear ask and the SOW. High level response after a quick read-through: we can likely do a scoped telemetry tag + config flag but need to validate a couple of platform constraints.

Questions to narrow scope: 
- Is the telemetry key strictly a single KV pair (tenant_sla_tag) or do they expect multiple attributes?
- Do they require UI controls in Console or is a config API + feature flag sufficient for procurement?

From a product perspective I want to avoid a long-lived custom branch. If we can ship a small, well-tested feature behind a flag and roll it to Dedicated only for NovaCorp, that's reasonable. But Eng needs estimated dev + QA time and a deployment window that doesn't conflict with the quarterly infra maintenance (first week of Dec).

Marissa / Karthik — can you triage feasibility and give a T-shirt estimate (S/M/L) + a best-case ETA for a canary deployment? If the estimate is >6w, we should list the minimal workaround and what procurement language it buys us.

Happy to join a quick 15-min triage at 10am PT.

Laura
Senior PM, Product — Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 13:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High-touch deal: feature accelerant & roadmap gating

Thanks — quick engineering readout. TL;DR: feasible as a scoped work item with a 3-week fast path + 2-week QA/rollout if we prioritize it for the next sprint and can deprioritize one medium ticket. Details below.

Feasibility notes:
- Telemetry: single kv pair tenant_sla_tag can be added to the existing trace enrichment middleware with negligible perf impact (we already add tenant_id). Implementation work: ~2 dev days + 1 code review.
- Config: expose tenant_sla_tag in Dedicated tenant config API and ensure rollout gating via existing feature-flagging infra: ~6 dev days (API + validation + migration guard).
- Console: not required for v1 — we can make this API-only and add Console later. Procurement confirmed earlier that API control is acceptable.
- QA & rollout: 5 working days for E2E tests, staging validation, and a single-tenant canary on Dedicated. We'll need a small infra window to apply the Dedicated config for the canary (low risk).

Estimated schedule (best-case, assuming immediate prioritization):
- Dev sprint start: 2026-10-25
- Dev complete: 2026-11-10
- QA & staging: 2026-11-11 — 2026-11-17
- Canary rollout & monitor: 2026-11-18 — 2026-11-24
- Production-ready for broader enablement: 2026-11-25 + final compliance sign-off

If this timeline is acceptable I will flag this as a P0 spike for the infra backlog and align with SRE for the canary window. Workaround if we cannot ship in time: provide a detailed observability runbook + manual tagging via our support ops team (requires a signed SLA amendment that ELA/Legal approves). That workaround buys ~4–6 weeks but is operationally heavier for NovaCorp.

Marissa
Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: traffic-matrix.csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elaine Torres <elaine.torres@novacorp.com>, Procurement <procurement@novacorp.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 14:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High-touch deal: feature accelerant & roadmap gating

Appreciate the quick triage and the clear timeline — this is great. Customer update and ask: Elaine confirms API-only is fine for v1 and that signing is contingent on a production-ready ship no later than 2026-11-30.

Proposal to close the loop:
- Product/Eng: confirm we can target the Marissa schedule and mark the work as a P0 spike for the next sprint (Ben will escalate to sales leadership if we need additional priority).
- CS/Sales (Ben): I will offer NovaCorp a 30-day activation credit and a short onboarding engagement if they sign now and we ship the canary by 11/24.
- Legal/Finance: I will work the SOW to reflect the temporary manual tagging option as a fallback and include a target delivery date; please flag any billing/contract implications.

If everyone agrees I propose a short all-hands sync tomorrow 10am PT to lock prioritization and confirm SRE window. I'll prepare a brief one-pager for leadership and include the risk/benefit tradeoffs.

Ben
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

title: Gilmore Analytics: production spike after model v3.1-quant rollout
preview:
From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Aditya —

We pushed your v3.1-quant model yesterday evening (kanban release window) and starting overnight we’re seeing a major change in production: token p95 latency is ~5x baseline, and our request error rate climbed from ~0.5% to ~12%. These errors are decode-time timeouts and some "model not available" during peak bursts. This is affecting our customer-facing summarization flows; we’ve paused non-critical jobs, but the real-time API used by our mobile app is impacted and we risk churn.

Attached: a short sample of error logs and a capture of a failed request (gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log). We rotated keys and retried to rule out auth. The only change on our side was flipping to the new model tag per your docs.

We need an urgent triage and a rollback option. We’re also going to trigger procurement/finance if SLA credits are on the table — this is material.

Can we get a timeboxed remediation plan and a firm ETA for either a patch or rollback? We can make time for a call now if helpful.

— Elena
Customer Success Lead
Gilmore Analytics


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Elena — thanks for the heads up and for sending the logs. I’m very sorry for the disruption. We’ve flagged this as a Sev-1 and Marcus from inference ops is taking lead on triage.

Immediate plan:
- Marcus will start an investigation focused on the quantization profile we shipped with v3.1 and the runtime kernel selection that runs on your Dedicated pool.
- If we confirm a regression, we will propose either a rollback to v3.0 (same variant you used last month) or a hotfix qprofile within 4–8 hours.
- I’ll coordinate a short call with your team and our eng/CS leadership in the next 60 minutes.

Marissa, Lauren — looping you in for commercial context and to start the credit/contract thread if needed.

Elena — attaching the logs to our ticket and will update this thread when we have a root-cause hypothesis.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

[attachment: gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Quick triage notes (initial):
- Reproduced locally against a subset of the sample requests. The v3.1-quant qprofile enables an aggressive KV-cache compaction heuristic intended to reduce memory, but it seems to increase cache churn under your real-time burst pattern. That churn causes additional KV re-fetches and kernel re-init, pushing decode p95 up and occasionally tripping our request timeout fallback (hence the 5–15% observed timeouts).
- Error messages in logs point to "kv-cache-miss -> deferred fetch" sequences followed by longer kernel scheduling delays on your GPU pool (Dedicated config: g5x-large).

Proposed immediate actions: 
1) Hot rollback to model:v3.0 (non-quantized or the v3.0-qsafe profile) for your production route. This is the least risky; we can do a targeted swap on your route in ~25 minutes with no config change required.
2) Parallel: prepare a conservative qprofile for v3.1 (v3.1-qconservative) that disables the compaction heuristic and re-test in canary. Target 48-hour turnaround for validated qprofile.
3) If you prefer, we can enable adaptive per-customer kv thresholds (config flip) while we iterate — adds ~2 hours to cutover but avoids full model swap if you want to remain on v3.1.

I recommend option 1 for fastest user impact remediation. I’ll start the rollback immediately if I get an approval from Aditya/Elena.

— Marcus
Senior Inference Engineer
Redwood Inference

[attachment: profile-v3.1-qprofile.json]

On Sun, Oct 18, 2026 at 09:42 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Elena — thanks for the heads up and for sending the logs. I’m very sorry for the disruption. We’ve flagged this as a Sev-1 and Marcus from inference ops is taking lead on triage.


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Team —

Thank you Marcus for the quick analysis. Elena, we fully understand the impact and want to move decisively. Commercially, I am prepared to approve a one-off goodwill credit for the affected window pending final impact numbers; Marissa will draft the credit terms and start the approval path with Finance. We’ll also prepare an executive one-pager for your leadership.

Operational ask: Aditya, please approve Marcus to execute the rollback to v3.0 on Gilmore’s production route. We want to make that swap as soon as Marcus confirms deployment steps and a brief smoke test plan. After rollback, we will run an extended monitor for 72 hours and deliver an exec-impact summary. If rollback is insufficient we’ll schedule a deeper session and consider an expedited patch.

I’m copying Rishi (Head of Product Ops) to flag that our model rollout gating and canary controls need a short-term hardening item — we should avoid automatic mass flips when qprofile changes are included. Rishi, please advise on immediate policy changes we can apply to Dedicated customers.

— Lauren Bishop
Director, Customer Success Operations
Redwood Inference

[attachment: exec-impact-summary-20261019.pdf]

From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>, Procurement <procurement@gilmore.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

All — thanks for the quick mobilization. We authorize the rollback to v3.0 now. Please confirm:
- ETA for rollback start and smoke test window
- Expected user-visible recovery time (ballpark)
- Who should be on the post-rollback call (we’ll have engineering + product + procurement)

Regarding credits: procurement wants a clear calculation of affected requests and time window; Marissa, please send a proposed credits spreadsheet and the contract clause you plan to reference. We will move the renewal conversation slightly — we want to see the 72-hour monitor before finalizing, but this is high priority for our renewal team.

We appreciate the transparency and look forward to the rollback.

— Elena
Customer Success Lead
Gilmore Analytics

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-12T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Request for BCP artifacts for SIG v2.1: MercuryHealth

Hi Ava,

As part of our SIG assessment (SIG v2.1) we need a small package of BCP/DR artifacts to validate your recovery commitments. Specifically we are asking for:
- Your current BCP/runbook that covers multi-region failover scenarios
- RTO/RPO attestation for the hosted Redwood API and for model serving (per region if available)
- Evidence of a most-recent DR test (logs or summary) showing the scenario, date, and test outcomes
- SOC 2 control snapshot or pointer to the control owner we can speak with

If you can provide redacted copies or a secure Drive link that would be best. Target turnaround for MercuryHealth is EOW (10/16) to keep our procurement timeline. If something will take longer please let me know which items you can supply this week and which will need more time.

Thanks,
Claire Hammond
Security Operations
MercuryHealth
claire.h@mercuryhealth.com

---
Attachments: none


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Hammond <claire.h@mercuryhealth.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-12T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for BCP artifacts for SIG v2.1: MercuryHealth

Hi Claire — thanks for the clear list. We can provide the runbook, an RTO/RPO attestation, and the DR test summary.

Quick notes:
- We will redact internal IP addresses and on-call phone numbers before sending.
- SOC 2 control snapshots are available; I will loop in our SOC2 owner if you want the unredacted evidence under an NDA.
- If you need a walkthrough rather than static artifacts, we can schedule a 30-minute call with our ops team next week.

I am assigning Diego to assemble the packet and Karthik to verify the RTO/RPO numbers against the latest capacity matrix. Expect a secure Drive share by 10/15.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124

Attachments: none


From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-13T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: BCP artifacts — assembling packet

Ava / Karthik — forwarding Claire's ask. Plan and questions below.

Plan:
1) Pull the BCP runbook (redwood_bcp_runbook_v2_1.pdf) and run a scrub for customer-facing distribution. Redact internal hostnames, raw IPs, and vendor contact numbers.
2) Include RTO/RPO attestation (rto_rpo_attestation_2026-09.pdf) that lists committed targets for Hosted API (us-west-2, eu-west-1) and Dedicated pools. Karthik — please confirm the numbers and add a one-line note where the RPO differs by model type.
3) Attach the last DR test summary (dr_test_log_20260520.csv) with a high-level CSV summary and a one-page narrative of the test scenario and outcomes.
4) Add SOC2 control snapshot (soc2_control_snapshot_2026-09.zip) but mark it as 'NDA required' in the Drive share and include contact for control owner (Monica).

Questions:
- Do we want to include raw KV cache behavior or just the high-level failover steps? I'd recommend high-level only.
- Any special redaction rules for MercuryHealth (PII masking beyond standard redaction)?

I will prep the files and upload to a restricted Drive link. Ava — want me to send the Drive link to Claire directly or have you send from your mailbox?

- Diego
Site Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference
diego.fernandez@redwood.ai

---
Attachments: dr_test_log_20260520.csv (stub), redwood_bcp_runbook_v2_1.pdf (stub)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-14T10:11:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: BCP artifacts — assembling packet

Diego — confirmed RTO/RPO values:
- Hosted API (standard gen models): RTO = 60 minutes (regional failover), RPO = 5 minutes (KV cache snapshots + prefix caching)
- Dedicated (reserved pools): RTO = 30 minutes for pre-warmed pools, RPO = 1 minute for checkpointed state where available
- Note: for long-running streaming sessions there are graceful-degradation recommendations rather than hard RPO guarantees; include that caveat in the attestation.

I also recommend we redact any per-customer test IDs and remove raw ticket links. Monica can confirm SOC2 distribution constraints.

I'll add the attestation document and a short bullet explaining the model variance.

Karthik
Head of Platform Reliability (interim)
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

---
Attachments: rto_rpo_attestation_2026-09.pdf (stub)

Date: 2026-10-20T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: [SIG v2.1] Request for BCP artifacts for SIG v2.1: MercuryHealth

Claire — attaching the packet we discussed. Links below point to a restricted Drive folder; files are redacted per our standard distribution rules. If MercuryHealth needs unredacted SOC 2 artifacts we can provide them under your NDA — Monica is the control owner and copied.

Drive share (restricted): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cExampleBCPFolder
Files uploaded: redwood_bcp_runbook_v2_1.pdf (redacted), rto_rpo_attestation_2026-09.pdf, dr_test_log_20260520.csv (summary only), soc2_control_snapshot_2026-09.zip (NDA required)

Follow-ups/options:
- We can host a 30-minute walkthrough with our SRE and compliance owners (Diego + Monica) the week of 10/26.
- If you have a template attestation form MercuryHealth prefers, send it and we'll return a signed PDF.

Thanks — please confirm receipt so I can update the HubSpot record (HUB-98751) and mark this item as delivered.

Best,
Ava Chen

title: Employee access & training evidence for SOC2 review - BrightLeaf
preview:
From: Tracy Mills <tracy.mills@auditaudit.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Rao <samir@brightleafhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SOC2 request - onboarding/offboarding evidence (BrightLeaf)

Hi Ben,

Thanks for taking this. As part of BrightLeaf's SOC2 readiness review we need the following artifacts for employees in scope (all RedLeaf-facing product & support accounts):

1) Training roster: employee id, role, email, training module ids completed, completion timestamps, and the training policy version in effect. CSV export preferred.
2) Background check confirmations: redacted attestations (name + last four of SSN or unique identifier) and check date for hires since 2025-01-01.
3) Deprovisioning log: list of accounts terminated/disabled in the past 18 months with date/time, principal who performed the action, and ticket/reference id.
4) MFA enrollment snapshot: users with MFA enforced and enforcement date.

Can you deliver these items by COB Pacific on Tue Oct 20? If some items require longer redaction / legal review please flag them and provide an interim summary.

If possible, provide the artifacts as an archive on a short-lived signed URL (1 hour) and include a checksum. We will treat the files as confidential and return confirmation when validated.

Best,
Tracy Mills
Senior Audit Manager
Auditaudit Compliance Services

---
Attachment: requested_fields_template.csv (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tracy Mills <tracy.mills@auditaudit.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 09:28:34 -0700
Subject: Fwd: SOC2 request - onboarding/offboarding evidence (BrightLeaf)

Team — forwarding BrightLeaf's ask (below). Short timeline. Laura, can you pull the training roster and MFA snapshot exports? Tessa, can you run the background-check confirmation report and pull the deprovisioning event logs from IAM and Okta for the last 18 months?

Notes / constraints:
- Redaction: Legal prefers redacting PII down to initials + last4, include a separate mapping index kept offline if auditors need it under NDA.
- Deprovisioning events live in two places (Okta admin audit + internal deprovisioning ticket system HS-9921). Merge by user email and timestamp.
- Use evidence-pack-v1.zip naming convention and sign the archive with SHA256.

Deadline: I told them COB Tue 10/20. If either export runs long, flag to me by EOD today and provide an interim schedule.

Thanks — Ben
Director, Enterprise Sales (covering BrightLeaf)
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Fwd: SOC2 request - onboarding/offboarding evidence (BrightLeaf)

Ben / Tessa / Tracy — quick update on artifacts:

1) Training roster: exported a CSV (onboarding_training_roster_2025-2026.csv). Fields: employee_email, role, hire_date, training_module_id, module_name, completed_at, policy_version. Sample rows included in archive.

2) MFA snapshot: extracted via Okta API; file included as mfa_enrollment_snapshot_2026-10-19.csv. We mark enforcement dates where available.

3) Background checks: Tessa and I pulled the redacted confirmations. One exception: contractor \"Jordan Miles\" (contractor id C-219) joined 2025-09-03 — background provider shows incomplete due to vendor mismatch. We're escalating; expected remediation by 10/22. Included background_checks_redacted_2025-2026.csv (redacted to initials + last4).

4) Deprovisioning events: combined Okta admin audit + HS tickets. File: deprovisioning_events_2026-10-01_to_2026-10-19.csv. Sample contents below (first two rows):

user_email,action,timestamp,performed_by,ticket_id
jordan.miles@vendormail.com,account_disabled,2026-09-28T14:03:21Z,sysadmin1@redwood.ai,HS-8472
melissa.choi@redwood.ai,access_revoked,2026-10-05T02:12:07Z,iam-automation@redwood.ai,HS-9123

We compressed these into evidence-pack-v1.zip and applied SHA256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb924... (truncated for email). Uploading signed URL now; see the link below.

Let me know if you need the full PII mapping (we'll provide only under executed NDA).

Laura Bennett
Senior Security Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tracy Mills <tracy.mills@brightleafhealth.com>, Samir Rao <samir@brightleafhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SOC2 request - onboarding/offboarding evidence (BrightLeaf)

Tracy / Samir — attaching the package requested. Delivery details: 

- Evidence archive (evidence-pack-v1.zip) uploaded to a signed S3 URL (1 hour expiry): https://s3.redwoodinternal.com/evidence/BRL-0447/evidence-pack-v1.zip?X-Amz-Expires=3600
- Files inside: onboarding_training_roster_2025-2026.csv, background_checks_redacted_2025-2026.csv, mfa_enrollment_snapshot_2026-10-19.csv, deprovisioning_events_2026-10-01_to_2026-10-19.csv
- SHA256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 (full digest in the signed metadata file).

Redactions: PII redacted to initials + last4 as discussed. We have an offline mapping for auditors under NDA; if Auditaudit needs the mapping for a sample of users, please request it and we will set up an NDA + secure transfer.

Open items: contractor Jordan Miles (C-219) shows background incomplete due to vendor mismatch; remediation ETA 2026-10-22. If you need earlier evidence we can provide the in-progress vendor report (redacted).

Please confirm receipt and whether you need additional items (IAM role snapshots, service account entitlement matrix, or longer deprovisioning history).

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Hi Ava / Suresh —

Thanks again for a productive call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we’re all aligned: 

Key takeaways
- CloudNova is evaluating inference options for a customer-facing summarization pipeline (200–600 tokens per request, heavy tail to 2k tokens for long transcripts).
- Primary concerns: 99th-pct latency under 400ms for short calls, predictable per-token cost, and hallucination risk on financial entities.

- Cost per 1k tokens within 1.2x of current hyperscaler after enabling Redwood Optimize suggestions.

title: Clarify billing terms: tokens / characters / requests + caching impact
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>, Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Alex/Priya — thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and two clarifying asks so we can produce a short pricing sketch for your CFO.

Recap (very short):
- You run the transaction-monitoring assistant for merchant support and expect heavy read-style prompts (same templates, different variables) + some freeform investigator prompts.
- Key unknowns for our pricing sketch: monthly request volume, average length (tokens vs characters), and rough budget target. You mentioned you were trying to reconcile metrics internally (tokens vs characters vs API requests) — that’s common.

Can you confirm two things so we can produce a short decision-grade spreadsheet:
1) Ballpark monthly request range (best guess): low / mid / high (e.g., 500k / 2.5M / 5M)
2) Target spend range (monthly): e.g., $10k–$15k or $40k–$60k — whatever you’re comfortable sharing.

One quick note on caching: you mentioned ~40% of traffic might be identical prompts (templated responses). Do you mean identical full prompts and expected outputs (safe to cache whole response), or identical prompt prefixes where the variable suffix changes? The difference matters for billable tokens.

Attached: a tiny estimate workbook (fintect-monthly-estimates.xlsx) and a one-pager on how we compute tokens (redwood-1m-token-breakdown.pdf).

If it helps, I can run three scenarios and send a short 1-page recommendation before a follow-up call — would a 30-minute slot tomorrow morning (10/19 10:00am PT) work?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: fintect-monthly-estimates.xlsx)
(Attachment: redwood-1m-token-breakdown.pdf)

From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Thanks Ben — quick answers below. Appreciate the clear recap.

1) Monthly request estimate (internal convos):
- Low: ~700k requests/mo
- Mid: ~2.5M requests/mo (most likely)
- High: ~5M requests/mo (peak / holiday spikes)

2) Budget: our procurement window is tight; the initial target is $10k–$15k/month. We may expand after an ROI pilot, but that’s the starting point.

Cache detail: when I said ~40% identical, I meant the user-facing prompt template + variables are identical in ~40% cases — the outputs are usually identical or close enough that caching the template+variables would produce a correct cached response. Investigator prompts are unique.

Regarding tokens/characters: we’ve been tracking by characters in our logs; average prompt length (characters) is ~400 chars (variable text included). We don’t have tokenized numbers yet. If you can convert 400 chars -> tokens using a rough rule-of-thumb in your scenarios that would be helpful.

Happy to take a call tomorrow 10/19 10am PT — Alex and I will join.

Best,
Priya Sengupta
Head of Product
Fintect Analytics


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Monica/Jordan — forwarding Priya’s answers. Below I’ve sketched a short, decision-focused breakdown we can share with Fintect ahead of tomorrow’s call. Please review and flag any corrections. I’ll paste the short calc and recommended levers.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

(see above)

--- End forwarded message ---

Quick glossary we should align on (short):
- Tokens: unit Redwood bills on — model input tokens + model output tokens (tokenizer-dependent).
- Characters: raw character count from customer logs — not billed directly, but we can convert.
- Requests: API calls; useful for operational/throughput cost but not the billing unit we charge customers on (billing is per token).
- Prompt caching: when a request matches a cached key and Redwood can serve a stored response, that saved work can reduce billed tokens depending on how the caching path is implemented (full-response cache vs prefix reuse). If we serve a cached full response without running the model, billed tokens can be near-zero for the cached requests (we may still bill a tiny metadata or retrieval fee depending on contract). If it’s a prefix cache that only saves part of the input, billed tokens reduce proportionally to the unrepeated portion.

Baseline numbers (for a quick, conservative sketch):
- Assumptions from Priya: mid volume = 2.5M requests/month; avg prompt length ~400 chars. For conversion we’ll use a conservative rule: 1 token ~= 4 characters -> avg prompt tokens = 100 tokens.
- Avg completion (expected output) for these assistant replies = ~200 tokens.
- Total tokens per request = 300 tokens.
- Monthly billed tokens (no caching) = 300 * 2.5M = 750M tokens/month.

Caching scenario (Priya: ~40% identical full prompts):
- If we can serve full cached responses for that 40% (i.e., no model run), billed tokens drop to 60% of baseline: 0.6 * 750M = 450M tokens/month.

Cost scenarios (customer target $10k–$15k/mo):
- At a $/1M-token rate = $150 -> 450M tokens -> 450 * $150 = $67,500/month (too high).
- To hit $15k/month on 450M tokens -> implied $/1M-token = $33.3.

Practical levers to reach customer budget:
1) Increase cache hit or cache more (engineering): move more templates to full-response cache; prioritize top-20 templates that represent ~70% of identical traffic.
2) Use smaller / quantized model for templated replies (reduce per-token unit price and output size).
3) Billing construct: blended pricing for templated traffic vs freeform investigator queries (e.g., fixed per-request for cached templates + per-token for unique queries).
4) Commitment/term discount: a monthly commit or 12mo pilot gives us leverage to offer a much lower effective $/1M-token.

Proposed next steps to share with Fintect before the call:
- I’ll prepare three 1-page scenarios (Conservative / Target / Stretch) with the assumptions above and highlight which levers get them to ~$10–15k/mo.
- Recommend we surface the top-20 templates and a short export so our infra folks can estimate achievable cache hit when implemented in production.

Monica/Jordan — if that math looks sane, I’ll send the 1-pager to Priya and Alex this morning and use it as the agenda for the call.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Ben / Priya — this is perfect, thanks for running the numbers so quickly. The conservative assumptions look fine for now.

We’ll join 10/19 10:00am PT — Priya will bring the export of the top templates (should be ready in the next hour). For the call, please include a simple scenario where commitment + caching + a smaller model gets us into the $10k–$15k range. If you can show an example blended structure (e.g., $X per cached request + $Y per 1M tokens for freeform), that will be the most useful for procurement.

See you in 40m.

Alex
VP, Partnerships
Fintect Analytics

Thanks Ben — quick answers below. Appreciate the clear recap.

1) Monthly request estimate (internal convos):
- Low: ~700k requests/mo
- Mid: ~2.5M requests/mo (most likely)
- High: ~5M requests/mo (peak / holiday spikes)

2) Budget: our procurement window is tight; the initial target is $10k–$15k/month. We may expand after an ROI pilot, but that’s the starting point.

Cache detail: when I said ~40% identical, I meant the user-facing prompt template + variables are identical in ~40% cases — the outputs are usually identical or close enough that caching the template+variables would produce a correct cached response. Investigator prompts are unique.

Regarding tokens/characters: we’ve been tracking by characters in our logs; average prompt length (characters) is ~400 chars (variable text included). We don’t have tokenized numbers yet. If you can convert 400 chars -> tokens using a rough rule-of-thumb in your scenarios that would be helpful.

Priya Sengupta
Head of Product
Fintect Analytics


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Monica/Jordan — forwarding Priya’s answers. Below I’ve sketched a short, decision-focused breakdown we can share with Fintect ahead of tomorrow’s call. Please review and flag any corrections. I’ll paste the short calc and recommended levers.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

(see above)

--- End forwarded message ---

Quick glossary we should align on (short):
- Tokens: unit Redwood bills on — model input tokens + model output tokens (tokenizer-dependent).
- Characters: raw character count from customer logs — not billed directly, but we can convert.
- Requests: API calls; useful for operational/throughput cost but not the billing unit we charge customers on (billing is per token).
- Prompt caching: when a request matches a cached key and Redwood can serve a stored response, that saved work can reduce billed tokens depending on how the caching path is implemented (full-response cache vs prefix reuse). If we serve a cached full response without running the model, billed tokens can be near-zero for the cached requests (we may still bill a tiny metadata or retrieval fee depending on contract). If it’s a prefix cache that only saves part of the input, billed tokens reduce proportionally to the unrepeated portion.

Baseline numbers (for a quick, conservative sketch):
- Assumptions from Priya: mid volume = 2.5M requests/month; avg prompt length ~400 chars. For conversion we’ll use a conservative rule: 1 token ~= 4 characters -> avg prompt tokens = 100 tokens.
- Avg completion (expected output) for these assistant replies = ~200 tokens.
- Total tokens per request = 300 tokens.
- Monthly billed tokens (no caching) = 300 * 2.5M = 750M tokens/month.

Caching scenario (Priya: ~40% identical full prompts):
- If we can serve full cached responses for that 40% (i.e., no model run), billed tokens drop to 60% of baseline: 0.6 * 750M = 450M tokens/month.

Cost scenarios (customer target $10k–$15k/mo):
- At a $/1M-token rate = $150 -> 450M tokens -> 450 * $150 = $67,500/month (too high).
- To hit $15k/month on 450M tokens -> implied $/1M-token = $33.3.

Practical levers to reach customer budget:
1) Increase cache hit or cache more (engineering): move more templates to full-response cache; prioritize top-20 templates that represent ~70% of identical traffic.
2) Use smaller / quantized model for templated replies (reduce per-token unit price and output size).
3) Billing construct: blended pricing for templated traffic vs freeform investigator queries (e.g., fixed per-request for cached templates + per-token for unique queries).
4) Commitment/term discount: a monthly commit or 12mo pilot gives us leverage to offer a much lower effective $/1M-token.

Proposed next steps to share with Fintect before the call:
- I’ll prepare three 1-page scenarios (Conservative / Target / Stretch) with the assumptions above and highlight which levers get them to ~$10–15k/mo.
- Recommend we surface the top-20 templates and a short export so our infra folks can estimate achievable cache hit when implemented in production.

Monica/Jordan — if that math looks sane, I’ll send the 1-pager to Priya and Alex this morning and use it as the agenda for the call.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
